   514→          sharedServer.metadataPublishingFaultHandler,
   515→          "broker",
   516→          tokenManager),
   517→        new AclPublisher(
   518→          config.nodeId,
   519→          sharedServer.metadataPublishingFaultHandler,
   520→          "broker",
   521→          authorizerPlugin.toJava
   522→        ),
   523→        sharedServer.initialBrokerMetadataLoadFaultHandler,
   524→        sharedServer.metadataPublishingFaultHandler
   525→      )
   526→      // If the BrokerLifecycleManager's initial catch-up future fails, it means we timed out
   527→      // or are shutting down before we could catch up. Therefore, also fail the firstPublishFuture.
   528→      lifecycleManager.initialCatchUpFuture.whenComplete((_, e) => {
   529→        if (e != null) brokerMetadataPublisher.firstPublishFuture.completeExceptionally(e)
   530→      })
   531→      metadataPublishers.add(brokerMetadataPublisher)
   532→      brokerRegistrationTracker = new BrokerRegistrationTracker(config.brokerId,
   533→        () => lifecycleManager.resendBrokerRegistration())
   534→      metadataPublishers.add(brokerRegistrationTracker)
   535→
   536→
   537→      // Register parts of the broker that can be reconfigured via dynamic configs.  This needs to
   538→      // be done before we publish the dynamic configs, so that we don't miss anything.
   539→      config.dynamicConfig.addReconfigurables(this)
   540→
   541→      // Install all the metadata publishers.
   542→      FutureUtils.waitWithLogging(logger.underlying, logIdent,
   543→        "the broker metadata publishers to be installed",
   544→        sharedServer.loader.installPublishers(metadataPublishers), startupDeadline, time)
   545→
   546→      // Wait for this broker to contact the quorum, and for the active controller to acknowledge
   547→      // us as caught up. It will do this by returning a heartbeat response with isCaughtUp set to
   548→      // true. The BrokerLifecycleManager tracks this.
   549→      FutureUtils.waitWithLogging(logger.underlying, logIdent,
   550→        "the controller to acknowledge that we are caught up",
   551→        lifecycleManager.initialCatchUpFuture, startupDeadline, time)
   552→
   553→      // Wait for the first metadata update to be published. Metadata updates are not published
   554→      // until we read at least up to the high water mark of the cluster metadata partition.
   555→      // Usually, we publish the initial metadata before lifecycleManager.initialCatchUpFuture
   556→      // is completed, so this check is not necessary. But this is a simple check to make
   557→      // completely sure.
   558→      FutureUtils.waitWithLogging(logger.underlying, logIdent,
   559→        "the initial broker metadata update to be published",
   560→        brokerMetadataPublisher.firstPublishFuture , startupDeadline, time)
   561→
   562→      // Now that we have loaded some metadata, we can log a reasonably up-to-date broker
   563→      // configuration.  Keep in mind that KafkaConfig.originals is a mutable field that gets set
   564→      // by the dynamic configuration publisher. Ironically, KafkaConfig.originals does not
   565→      // contain the original configuration values.
   566→      new KafkaConfig(config.originals(), true)
   567→
   568→      // We're now ready to unfence the broker. This also allows this broker to transition
   569→      // from RECOVERY state to RUNNING state, once the controller unfences the broker.
   570→      FutureUtils.waitWithLogging(logger.underlying, logIdent,
   571→        "the broker to be unfenced",
   572→        lifecycleManager.setReadyToUnfence(), startupDeadline, time)
   573→
   574→      // Enable inbound TCP connections. Each endpoint will be started only once its matching
   575→      // authorizer future is completed.
   576→      val endpointReadyFutures = {
   577→        val builder = new EndpointReadyFutures.Builder()
   578→        builder.build(authorizerPlugin.toJava,
   579→          new KafkaAuthorizerServerInfo(
   580→            new ClusterResource(clusterId),
   581→            config.nodeId,
   582→            listenerInfo.listeners().values(),
   583→            listenerInfo.firstListener(),
   584→            config.earlyStartListeners.map(_.value()).asJava))
   585→      }
   586→      val authorizerFutures = endpointReadyFutures.futures().asScala.toMap
   587→      val enableRequestProcessingFuture = socketServer.enableRequestProcessing(authorizerFutures)
   588→
   589→      // Block here until all the authorizer futures are complete.
   590→      FutureUtils.waitWithLogging(logger.underlying, logIdent,
   591→        "all of the authorizer futures to be completed",
   592→        CompletableFuture.allOf(authorizerFutures.values.toSeq: _*), startupDeadline, time)
   593→
   594→      // Wait for all the SocketServer ports to be open, and the Acceptors to be started.
   595→      FutureUtils.waitWithLogging(logger.underlying, logIdent,
   596→        "all of the SocketServer Acceptors to be started",
   597→        enableRequestProcessingFuture, startupDeadline, time)
   598→
   599→      maybeChangeStatus(STARTING, STARTED)
   600→    } catch {
   601→      case e: Throwable =>
   602→        maybeChangeStatus(STARTING, STARTED)
   603→        fatal("Fatal error during broker startup. Prepare to shutdown", e)
   604→        shutdown()
   605→        throw if (e.isInstanceOf[ExecutionException]) e.getCause else e
   606→    }
   607→  }

   609→  private def createGroupCoordinator(): GroupCoordinator = {
   610→    // Create group coordinator, but don't start it until we've started replica manager.
   611→    // Hardcode Time.SYSTEM for now as some Streams tests fail otherwise, it would be good
   612→    // to fix the underlying issue.
   613→    val time = Time.SYSTEM
   614→    val serde = new GroupCoordinatorRecordSerde
   615→    val timer = new SystemTimerReaper(
   616→      "group-coordinator-reaper",
   617→      new SystemTimer("group-coordinator")
   618→    )
   619→    val loader = new CoordinatorLoaderImpl[CoordinatorRecord](
   620→      time,
   621→      replicaManager,
   622→      serde,
   623→      config.groupCoordinatorConfig.offsetsLoadBufferSize,
   624→      CoordinatorLoaderImpl.DEFAULT_COMMIT_INTERVAL_OFFSETS
   625→    )
   626→    val writer = new CoordinatorPartitionWriter(
   627→      replicaManager
   628→    )
   629→    new GroupCoordinatorService.Builder(config.brokerId, config.groupCoordinatorConfig)
   630→      .withTime(time)
   631→      .withTimer(timer)
   632→      .withLoader(loader)
   633→      .withWriter(writer)
   634→      .withCoordinatorRuntimeMetrics(new GroupCoordinatorRuntimeMetrics(metrics))
   635→      .withGroupCoordinatorMetrics(new GroupCoordinatorMetrics(KafkaYammerMetrics.defaultRegistry, metrics))
   636→      .withGroupConfigManager(groupConfigManager)
   637→      .withPersister(persister)
   638→      .withAuthorizerPlugin(authorizerPlugin.toJava)
   639→      .build()
   640→  }
   641→
   642→  private def createShareCoordinator(): ShareCoordinator = {
   643→    val time = Time.SYSTEM
   644→    val timer = new SystemTimerReaper(
   645→      "share-coordinator-reaper",

   647→    )
   648→
   649→    val serde = new ShareCoordinatorRecordSerde
   650→    val loader = new CoordinatorLoaderImpl[CoordinatorRecord](
   651→      time,
   652→      replicaManager,
   653→      serde,
   654→      config.shareCoordinatorConfig.shareCoordinatorLoadBufferSize(),
   655→      CoordinatorLoaderImpl.DEFAULT_COMMIT_INTERVAL_OFFSETS
   656→    )
   657→    val writer = new CoordinatorPartitionWriter(

   659→    )
   660→    new ShareCoordinatorService.Builder(config.brokerId, config.shareCoordinatorConfig)
   661→      .withTimer(timer)

   663→      .withLoader(loader)

   665→      .withCoordinatorRuntimeMetrics(new ShareCoordinatorRuntimeMetrics(metrics))

   667→      .withShareGroupEnabledConfigSupplier(() => config.shareGroupConfig.isShareGroupEnabled)
   668→      .build()

   671→  private def createShareStatePersister(): Persister = {
   672→    if (config.shareGroupConfig.shareGroupPersisterClassName.nonEmpty) {
   673→      val klass = Utils.loadClass(config.shareGroupConfig.shareGroupPersisterClassName, classOf[Object]).asInstanceOf[Class[Persister]]
   674→
   675→      if (klass.getName.equals(classOf[DefaultStatePersister].getName)) {
   676→        klass.getConstructor(classOf[PersisterStateManager])
   677→          .newInstance(
   678→            new PersisterStateManager(
   679→              NetworkUtils.buildNetworkClient("Persister", config, metrics, Time.SYSTEM, new LogContext(s"[Persister broker=${config.brokerId}]")),
   680→              new ShareCoordinatorMetadataCacheHelperImpl(metadataCache, key => shareCoordinator.partitionFor(key), config.interBrokerListenerName),
   681→              Time.SYSTEM,
   682→              new SystemTimerReaper(
   683→                "persister-state-manager-reaper",
   684→                new SystemTimer("persister")
   685→              )
   686→            )
   687→          )
   688→      } else if (klass.getName.equals(classOf[NoOpStatePersister].getName)) {
   689→        info("Using no-op persister")
   690→        new NoOpStatePersister()
   691→      } else {
   692→        error("Unknown persister specified. Persister is only factory-pluggable!")
   693→        throw new IllegalArgumentException("Unknown persister specified " + config.shareGroupConfig.shareGroupPersisterClassName)
   694→      }
   695→    } else {
   696→      // in case share coordinator not enabled or persister class name deliberately empty (key=)
   697→      info("Using no-op persister")
   698→      new NoOpStatePersister()
   699→    }
   700→  }
   701→
   702→  protected def createRemoteLogManager(listenerInfo: ListenerInfo): Option[RemoteLogManager] = {
   703→    if (config.remoteLogManagerConfig.isRemoteStorageSystemEnabled) {
   704→      val listenerName = config.remoteLogManagerConfig.remoteLogMetadataManagerListenerName()
   705→      val endpoint = if (listenerName != null) {
   706→        Some(listenerInfo.listeners().values().stream
   707→          .filter(e => ListenerName.normalised(e.listener()).equals(ListenerName.normalised(listenerName)))
   708→          .findFirst()
   709→          .orElseThrow(() => new ConfigException(RemoteLogManagerConfig.REMOTE_LOG_METADATA_MANAGER_LISTENER_NAME_PROP,
   710→            listenerName, "Should be set as a listener name within valid broker listener name list: " + listenerInfo.listeners().values())))
   711→      } else {
   712→        None
   713→      }
   714→
   715→      val rlm = new RemoteLogManager(config.remoteLogManagerConfig, config.brokerId, config.logDirs.get(0), clusterId, time,
   716→        (tp: TopicPartition) => logManager.getLog(tp).toJava,
   717→        (tp: TopicPartition, remoteLogStartOffset: java.lang.Long) => {
   718→          logManager.getLog(tp).foreach { log =>
   719→            log.updateLogStartOffsetFromRemoteTier(remoteLogStartOffset)
   720→          }
   721→        },
   722→        brokerTopicStats, metrics, endpoint.toJava)
   723→      Some(rlm)
   724→    } else {
   725→      None
   726→    }
   727→  }
   728→
   729→  override def shutdown(timeout: Duration): Unit = {
   730→    if (!maybeChangeStatus(STARTED, SHUTTING_DOWN)) return
   731→    try {
   732→      val deadline = time.milliseconds() + timeout.toMillis
   733→      info("shutting down")
   734→
   735→      if (config.controlledShutdownEnable) {
   736→        if (replicaManager != null)
   737→          replicaManager.beginControlledShutdown()
   738→
   739→        if (lifecycleManager != null) {
   740→          lifecycleManager.beginControlledShutdown()
   741→          try {
   742→            val controlledShutdownTimeoutMs = deadline - time.milliseconds()
   743→            lifecycleManager.controlledShutdownFuture.get(controlledShutdownTimeoutMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)
   744→          } catch {
   745→            case _: TimeoutException =>
   746→              error("Timed out waiting for the controller to approve controlled shutdown")
   747→            case e: Throwable =>
   748→              error("Got unexpected exception waiting for controlled shutdown future", e)
   749→          }
   750→        }
   751→      }
   752→      if (lifecycleManager != null)
   753→        lifecycleManager.beginShutdown()
   754→
   755→      // Stop socket server to stop accepting any more connections and requests.
   756→      // Socket server will be shutdown towards the end of the sequence.
   757→      if (socketServer != null) {
   758→        CoreUtils.swallow(socketServer.stopProcessingRequests(), this)
   759→      }
   760→      metadataPublishers.forEach(p => sharedServer.loader.removeAndClosePublisher(p).get())
   761→      metadataPublishers.clear()
   762→      if (dataPlaneRequestHandlerPool != null)
   763→        CoreUtils.swallow(dataPlaneRequestHandlerPool.shutdown(), this)
   764→      if (dataPlaneRequestProcessor != null)
   765→        CoreUtils.swallow(dataPlaneRequestProcessor.close(), this)
   766→      authorizerPlugin.foreach(Utils.closeQuietly(_, "authorizer plugin"))
   767→
   768→      /**
   769→       * We must shutdown the scheduler early because otherwise, the scheduler could touch other
   770→       * resources that might have been shutdown and cause exceptions.
   771→       * For example, if we didn't shutdown the scheduler first, when LogManager was closing
   772→       * partitions one by one, the scheduler might concurrently delete old segments due to
   773→       * retention. However, the old segments could have been closed by the LogManager, which would
   774→       * cause an IOException and subsequently mark logdir as offline. As a result, the broker would
   775→       * not flush the remaining partitions or write the clean shutdown marker. Ultimately, the
   776→       * broker would have to take hours to recover the log during restart.
   777→       */
   778→      if (kafkaScheduler != null)
   779→        CoreUtils.swallow(kafkaScheduler.shutdown(), this)
   780→
   781→      if (transactionCoordinator != null)
   782→        CoreUtils.swallow(transactionCoordinator.shutdown(), this)
   783→
   784→      if (groupConfigManager != null)
   785→        CoreUtils.swallow(groupConfigManager.close(), this)
   786→      if (groupCoordinator != null)
   787→        CoreUtils.swallow(groupCoordinator.shutdown(), this)
   788→      if (shareCoordinator != null)
   789→        CoreUtils.swallow(shareCoordinator.shutdown(), this)
   790→
   791→      if (assignmentsManager != null)
   792→        CoreUtils.swallow(assignmentsManager.close(), this)
   793→
   794→      if (replicaManager != null)
   795→        CoreUtils.swallow(replicaManager.shutdown(), this)
   796→
   797→      if (alterPartitionManager != null)
   798→        CoreUtils.swallow(alterPartitionManager.shutdown(), this)
   799→
   800→      if (forwardingManager != null)
   801→        CoreUtils.swallow(forwardingManager.close(), this)
   802→
   803→      if (clientToControllerChannelManager != null)
   804→        CoreUtils.swallow(clientToControllerChannelManager.shutdown(), this)
   805→
   806→      if (logManager != null) {
   807→        val brokerEpoch = if (lifecycleManager != null) lifecycleManager.brokerEpoch else -1
   808→        CoreUtils.swallow(logManager.shutdown(brokerEpoch), this)
   809→      }
   810→
   811→      // Close remote log manager to give a chance to any of its underlying clients
   812→      // (especially in RemoteStorageManager and RemoteLogMetadataManager) to close gracefully.
   813→      remoteLogManagerOpt.foreach(Utils.closeQuietly(_, "remote log manager"))
   814→
   815→      if (quotaManagers != null)
   816→        CoreUtils.swallow(quotaManagers.shutdown(), this)
   817→
   818→      if (socketServer != null)
   819→        CoreUtils.swallow(socketServer.shutdown(), this)
   820→
   821→      Utils.closeQuietly(brokerTopicStats, "broker topic stats")
   822→      Utils.closeQuietly(sharePartitionManager, "share partition manager")
   823→
   824→      if (persister != null)
   825→        CoreUtils.swallow(persister.stop(), this)
   826→
   827→      isShuttingDown.set(false)
   828→
   829→      if (lifecycleManager != null)
   830→        CoreUtils.swallow(lifecycleManager.close(), this)
   831→
   832→      CoreUtils.swallow(config.dynamicConfig.clear(), this)
   833→      Utils.closeQuietly(clientMetricsManager, "client metrics manager")
   834→      sharedServer.stopForBroker()
   835→      info("shut down completed")
   836→    } catch {
   837→      case e: Throwable =>
   838→        fatal("Fatal error during broker shutdown.", e)
   839→        throw e
   840→    } finally {
   841→      maybeChangeStatus(SHUTTING_DOWN, SHUTDOWN)
   842→    }
   843→  }
   844→
   845→  override def isShutdown(): Boolean = {
   846→    status == SHUTDOWN || status == SHUTTING_DOWN
   847→  }
   848→
   849→  override def awaitShutdown(): Unit = {
   850→    lock.lock()
   851→    try {
   852→      while (true) {
   853→        if (status == SHUTDOWN) return
   854→        awaitShutdownCond.awaitUninterruptibly()
   855→      }
   856→    } finally {
   857→      lock.unlock()
   858→    }
   859→  }
   860→
   861→  override def boundPort(listenerName: ListenerName): Int = socketServer.boundPort(listenerName)
   862→
   863→}
   864→

    14→ * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    15→ * limitations under the License.
    16→ */
    17→package kafka.server
    18→
    19→import java.util
    20→import java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit.{MILLISECONDS, NANOSECONDS}
    21→import java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture
    22→import kafka.utils.Logging
    23→import org.apache.kafka.clients.ClientResponse
    24→import org.apache.kafka.common.Uuid
    25→import org.apache.kafka.common.message.BrokerRegistrationRequestData.ListenerCollection
    26→import org.apache.kafka.common.message.{BrokerHeartbeatRequestData, BrokerRegistrationRequestData}
    27→import org.apache.kafka.common.protocol.Errors
    28→import org.apache.kafka.common.requests.{BrokerHeartbeatRequest, BrokerHeartbeatResponse, BrokerRegistrationRequest, BrokerRegistrationResponse}
    29→import org.apache.kafka.metadata.{BrokerState, VersionRange}
    30→import org.apache.kafka.queue.EventQueue.DeadlineFunction
    31→import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.{LogContext, Time}
    32→import org.apache.kafka.queue.{EventQueue, KafkaEventQueue}
    33→import org.apache.kafka.server.common.{ControllerRequestCompletionHandler, NodeToControllerChannelManager}
    34→
    35→import java.util.{Comparator, OptionalLong}
    36→import scala.collection.mutable
    37→import scala.jdk.CollectionConverters._
    38→
    39→/**
    40→ * The broker lifecycle manager owns the broker state.
    41→ *
    42→ * Its inputs are messages passed in from other parts of the broker and from the
    43→ * controller: requests to start up, or shut down, for example. Its output are the broker
    44→ * state and various futures that can be used to wait for broker state transitions to
    45→ * occur.
    46→ *
    47→ * The lifecycle manager handles registering the broker with the controller, as described
    48→ * in KIP-631. After registration is complete, it handles sending periodic broker
    49→ * heartbeats and processing the responses.
    50→ *
    51→ * This code uses an event queue paradigm. Modifications get translated into events, which
    52→ * are placed on the queue to be processed sequentially. As described in the JavaDoc for
    53→ * each variable, most mutable state can be accessed only from that event queue thread.
    54→ * In some cases we expose a volatile variable which can be read from any thread, but only
    55→ * written from the event queue thread.
    56→ */
    57→class BrokerLifecycleManager(
    58→  val config: KafkaConfig,
    59→  val time: Time,
    60→  val threadNamePrefix: String,
    61→  val logDirs: Set[Uuid],
    62→  val shutdownHook: () => Unit = () => {}
    63→) extends Logging {
    64→
    65→  private def logPrefix(): String = {
    66→    val builder = new StringBuilder("[BrokerLifecycleManager")
    67→    builder.append(" id=").append(config.nodeId)
    68→    builder.append("] ")
    69→    builder.toString()
    70→  }
    71→
    72→  val logContext = new LogContext(logPrefix())
    73→
    74→  this.logIdent = logContext.logPrefix()
    75→
    76→  /**
    77→   * The broker id.
    78→   */
    79→  private val nodeId = config.nodeId
    80→
    81→  /**
    82→   * The broker rack, or null if there is no configured rack.
    83→   */
    84→  private val rack = config.rack
    85→
    86→  /**
    87→   * How long to wait for registration to succeed before failing the startup process.
    88→   */
    89→  private val initialTimeoutNs =
    90→    MILLISECONDS.toNanos(config.initialRegistrationTimeoutMs.longValue())
    91→
    92→  /**
    93→   * The broker incarnation ID.  This ID uniquely identifies each time we start the broker
    94→   */
    95→  val incarnationId: Uuid = Uuid.randomUuid()
    96→
    97→  /**
    98→   * A future which is completed just as soon as the broker has caught up with the latest
    99→   * metadata offset for the first time.
   100→   */
   101→  val initialCatchUpFuture: CompletableFuture[Void] = new CompletableFuture[Void]()
   102→
   103→  /**
   104→   * A future which is completed when the broker is unfenced for the first time.
   105→   */
   106→  val initialUnfenceFuture: CompletableFuture[Void] = new CompletableFuture[Void]()
   107→
   108→  /**
   109→   * A future which is completed when controlled shutdown is done.
   110→   */
   111→  val controlledShutdownFuture: CompletableFuture[Void] = new CompletableFuture[Void]()
   112→
   113→  /**
   114→   * The broker epoch, or -1 if the broker has not yet registered.  This variable can only
   115→   * be written from the event queue thread.
   116→   */
   117→  @volatile private var _brokerEpoch = -1L
   118→
   119→  /**
   120→   * The current broker state.  This variable can only be written from the event queue
   121→   * thread.
   122→   */
   123→  @volatile private var _state = BrokerState.NOT_RUNNING
   124→
   125→  /**
   126→   * A thread-safe callback function which gives this manager the current highest metadata
   127→   * offset.  This variable can only be read or written from the event queue thread.
   128→   */
   129→  private var _highestMetadataOffsetProvider: () => Long = _
   130→
   131→  /**
   132→   * True only if we are ready to unfence the broker.  This variable can only be read or
   133→   * written from the event queue thread.
   134→   */
   135→  private var readyToUnfence = false
   136→
   137→  /**
   138→   * Map of accumulated offline directories. The value is true if the directory couldn't be communicated
   139→   * to the Controller.
   140→   * This variable can only be read or written from the event queue thread.
   141→   */
   142→  private var offlineDirs = mutable.Map[Uuid, Boolean]()
   143→
   144→  /**
   145→   * True if we sent an event queue to the active controller requesting controlled
   146→   * shutdown.  This variable can only be read or written from the event queue thread.
   147→   */
   148→  private var gotControlledShutdownResponse = false
   149→
   150→  /**
   151→   * Whether or not this broker is registered with the controller quorum.
   152→   * This variable can only be read or written from the event queue thread.
   153→   */
   154→  private var registered = false
   155→
   156→  /**
   157→   * True if a request has been sent and a response or timeout has not yet been processed.
   158→   * This variable can only be read or written from the event queue thread.
   159→   */
   160→  private var communicationInFlight = false
   161→
   162→  /**
   163→   * True if we should schedule the next communication immediately. This is used to delay
   164→   * an immediate scheduling of a communication event if one is already in flight.
   165→   * This variable can only be read or written from the event queue thread.
   166→   */
   167→  private var nextSchedulingShouldBeImmediate = false
   168→
   169→  /**
   170→   * True if the initial registration succeeded.  This variable can only be read or
   171→   * written from the event queue thread.
   172→   */
   173→  private var initialRegistrationSucceeded = false
   174→
   175→  /**
   176→   * The cluster ID, or null if this manager has not been started yet.  This variable can
   177→   * only be read or written from the event queue thread.
   178→   */
   179→  private var _clusterId: String = _
   180→
   181→  /**
   182→   * The listeners which this broker advertises.  This variable can only be read or
   183→   * written from the event queue thread.
   184→   */
   185→  private var _advertisedListeners: ListenerCollection = _
   186→
   187→  /**
   188→   * The features supported by this broker.  This variable can only be read or written
   189→   * from the event queue thread.
   190→   */
   191→  private var _supportedFeatures: util.Map[String, VersionRange] = _
   192→
   193→  /**
   194→   * The channel manager, or null if this manager has not been started yet.  This variable
   195→   * can only be read or written from the event queue thread.
   196→   */
   197→  private var _channelManager: NodeToControllerChannelManager = _
   198→
   199→  /**
   200→   * The broker epoch from the previous run, or empty if the epoch is not found.
   201→   */
   202→  @volatile private var previousBrokerEpoch: OptionalLong = OptionalLong.empty()
   203→
   204→  /**
   205→   * The event queue.
   206→   */
   207→  private[server] val eventQueue = new KafkaEventQueue(time,
   208→    logContext,
   209→    threadNamePrefix + "lifecycle-manager-",
   210→    new ShutdownEvent())
   211→
   212→  /**
   213→   * Start the BrokerLifecycleManager.
   214→   *
   215→   * @param highestMetadataOffsetProvider Provides the current highest metadata offset.
   216→   * @param channelManager                The NodeToControllerChannelManager to use.
   217→   * @param clusterId                     The cluster ID.
   218→   * @param advertisedListeners           The advertised listeners for this broker.
   219→   * @param supportedFeatures             The features for this broker.
   220→   * @param previousBrokerEpoch           The broker epoch before the reboot.
   221→   *
   222→   */
   223→  def start(highestMetadataOffsetProvider: () => Long,
   224→            channelManager: NodeToControllerChannelManager,
   225→            clusterId: String,
   226→            advertisedListeners: ListenerCollection,
   227→            supportedFeatures: util.Map[String, VersionRange],
   228→            previousBrokerEpoch: OptionalLong): Unit = {
   229→    this.previousBrokerEpoch = previousBrokerEpoch
   230→    eventQueue.append(new StartupEvent(highestMetadataOffsetProvider,
   231→      channelManager, clusterId, advertisedListeners, supportedFeatures))
   232→  }
   233→
   234→  def setReadyToUnfence(): CompletableFuture[Void] = {
   235→    eventQueue.append(new SetReadyToUnfenceEvent())
   236→    initialUnfenceFuture
   237→  }
   238→
   239→  /**
   240→   * Propagate directory failures to the controller.
   241→   * @param directory The ID for the directory that failed.
   242→   */
   243→  def propagateDirectoryFailure(directory: Uuid, timeout: Long): Unit = {
   244→    eventQueue.append(new OfflineDirEvent(directory))
   245→    // If we can't communicate the offline directory to the controller, we should shut down.
   246→    eventQueue.scheduleDeferred("offlineDirFailure",
   247→      new DeadlineFunction(time.nanoseconds() + MILLISECONDS.toNanos(timeout)),
   248→      new OfflineDirBrokerFailureEvent(directory))
   249→  }
   250→
   251→  def resendBrokerRegistration(): Unit = {
   252→    eventQueue.append(new ResendBrokerRegistrationEvent())
   253→  }
   254→
   255→  private class ResendBrokerRegistrationEvent extends EventQueue.Event {
   256→    override def run(): Unit = {
   257→      registered = false
   258→      scheduleNextCommunicationImmediately()
   259→    }
   260→  }
   261→
   262→  def brokerEpoch: Long = _brokerEpoch
   263→
   264→  def state: BrokerState = _state
   265→
   266→  private class BeginControlledShutdownEvent extends EventQueue.Event {
   267→    override def run(): Unit = {
   268→      _state match {
   269→        case BrokerState.PENDING_CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN =>
   270→          info("Attempted to enter pending controlled shutdown state, but we are " +
   271→          "already in that state.")
   272→        case BrokerState.RUNNING =>
   273→          info("Beginning controlled shutdown.")
   274→          _state = BrokerState.PENDING_CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN
   275→          // Send the next heartbeat immediately in order to let the controller
   276→          // begin processing the controlled shutdown as soon as possible.
   277→          scheduleNextCommunicationImmediately()
   278→
   279→        case _ =>
   280→          info(s"Skipping controlled shutdown because we are in state ${_state}.")
   281→          beginShutdown()
   282→      }
   283→    }
   284→  }
   285→
   286→  /**
   287→   * Enter the controlled shutdown state if we are in RUNNING state.
   288→   * Or, if we're not running, shut down immediately.
   289→   */
   290→  def beginControlledShutdown(): Unit = {
   291→    eventQueue.append(new BeginControlledShutdownEvent())
   292→  }
   293→
   294→  /**
   295→   * Start shutting down the BrokerLifecycleManager, but do not block.
   296→   */
   297→  def beginShutdown(): Unit = {
   298→    eventQueue.beginShutdown("beginShutdown")
   299→  }
   300→
   301→  /**
   302→   * Shut down the BrokerLifecycleManager and block until all threads are joined.
   303→   */
   304→  def close(): Unit = {
   305→    beginShutdown()
   306→    eventQueue.close()
   307→  }
   308→
   309→  private class SetReadyToUnfenceEvent extends EventQueue.Event {
   310→    override def run(): Unit = {
   311→      readyToUnfence = true
   312→      scheduleNextCommunicationImmediately()
   313→    }
   314→  }
   315→
   316→  private class OfflineDirEvent(val dir: Uuid) extends EventQueue.Event {
   317→    override def run(): Unit = {
   318→      if (offlineDirs.isEmpty) {
   319→        offlineDirs = mutable.Map(dir -> false)
   320→      } else {
   321→        offlineDirs += (dir -> false)
   322→      }
   323→      if (registered) {
   324→        scheduleNextCommunicationImmediately()
   325→      }
   326→    }
   327→  }
   328→
   329→  private class OfflineDirBrokerFailureEvent(offlineDir: Uuid) extends EventQueue.Event {
   330→    override def run(): Unit = {
   331→      if (!offlineDirs.getOrElse(offlineDir, false)) {
   332→        error(s"Shutting down because couldn't communicate offline log dir $offlineDir with controllers")
   333→        shutdownHook()
   334→      }
   335→    }
   336→  }
   337→
   338→  private class StartupEvent(highestMetadataOffsetProvider: () => Long,
   339→                     channelManager: NodeToControllerChannelManager,
   340→                     clusterId: String,
   341→                     advertisedListeners: ListenerCollection,
   342→                     supportedFeatures: util.Map[String, VersionRange]) extends EventQueue.Event {
   343→    override def run(): Unit = {
   344→      _highestMetadataOffsetProvider = highestMetadataOffsetProvider
   345→      _channelManager = channelManager
   346→      _channelManager.start()
   347→      _state = BrokerState.STARTING
   348→      _clusterId = clusterId
   349→      _advertisedListeners = advertisedListeners.duplicate()
   350→      _supportedFeatures = new util.HashMap[String, VersionRange](supportedFeatures)
   351→      eventQueue.scheduleDeferred("initialRegistrationTimeout",
   352→        new DeadlineFunction(time.nanoseconds() + initialTimeoutNs),
   353→        new RegistrationTimeoutEvent())
   354→      sendBrokerRegistration()
   355→      info(s"Incarnation $incarnationId of broker $nodeId in cluster $clusterId " +
   356→        "is now STARTING.")
   357→    }
   358→  }
   359→
   360→  private def sendBrokerRegistration(): Unit = {
   361→    val features = new BrokerRegistrationRequestData.FeatureCollection()
   362→    _supportedFeatures.asScala.foreach {
   363→      case (name, range) => features.add(new BrokerRegistrationRequestData.Feature().
   364→        setName(name).
   365→        setMinSupportedVersion(range.min()).
   366→        setMaxSupportedVersion(range.max()))
   367→    }
   368→    val sortedLogDirs = new util.ArrayList[Uuid]
   369→    logDirs.foreach(sortedLogDirs.add)
   370→    sortedLogDirs.sort(new Comparator[Uuid]() {
   371→      override def compare(a: Uuid, b: Uuid): Int = a.compareTo(b)
   372→    })
   373→    val data = new BrokerRegistrationRequestData().
   374→        setBrokerId(nodeId).
   375→        setIsMigratingZkBroker(false).
   376→        setClusterId(_clusterId).
   377→        setFeatures(features).
   378→        setIncarnationId(incarnationId).
   379→        setListeners(_advertisedListeners).
   380→        setRack(rack.orNull).
   381→        setPreviousBrokerEpoch(previousBrokerEpoch.orElse(-1L)).
   382→        setLogDirs(sortedLogDirs)
   383→    if (isDebugEnabled) {
   384→      debug(s"Sending broker registration $data")
   385→    }
   386→    _channelManager.sendRequest(new BrokerRegistrationRequest.Builder(data),
   387→      new BrokerRegistrationResponseHandler())
   388→    communicationInFlight = true
   389→  }
   390→
   391→  // the response handler is not invoked from the event handler thread,
   392→  // so it is not safe to update state here, instead, schedule an event
   393→  // to continue handling the response on the event handler thread
   394→  private class BrokerRegistrationResponseHandler extends ControllerRequestCompletionHandler {
   395→    override def onComplete(response: ClientResponse): Unit = {
   396→      eventQueue.prepend(new BrokerRegistrationResponseEvent(response, false))
   397→    }
   398→
   399→    override def onTimeout(): Unit = {
   400→      info(s"Unable to register the broker because the RPC got timed out before it could be sent.")
   401→      eventQueue.prepend(new BrokerRegistrationResponseEvent(null, true))
   402→    }
   403→  }
   404→
   405→  private class BrokerRegistrationResponseEvent(response: ClientResponse, timedOut: Boolean) extends EventQueue.Event {
   406→    override def run(): Unit = {
   407→      communicationInFlight = false
   408→      if (timedOut) {
   409→        scheduleNextCommunicationAfterFailure()
   410→        return
   411→      }
   412→      if (response.authenticationException() != null) {
   413→        error(s"Unable to register broker $nodeId because of an authentication exception.",
   414→          response.authenticationException())
   415→        scheduleNextCommunicationAfterFailure()
   416→      } else if (response.versionMismatch() != null) {
   417→        error(s"Unable to register broker $nodeId because of an API version problem.",
   418→          response.versionMismatch())
   419→        scheduleNextCommunicationAfterFailure()
   420→      } else if (response.responseBody() == null) {
   421→        warn(s"Unable to register broker $nodeId.")
   422→        scheduleNextCommunicationAfterFailure()
   423→      } else if (!response.responseBody().isInstanceOf[BrokerRegistrationResponse]) {
   424→        error(s"Unable to register broker $nodeId because the controller returned an " +
   425→          "invalid response type.")
   426→        scheduleNextCommunicationAfterFailure()
   427→      } else {
   428→        val message = response.responseBody().asInstanceOf[BrokerRegistrationResponse]
   429→        val errorCode = Errors.forCode(message.data().errorCode())
   430→        if (errorCode == Errors.NONE) {
   431→          _brokerEpoch = message.data().brokerEpoch()
   432→          registered = true
   433→          initialRegistrationSucceeded = true
   434→          info(s"Successfully registered broker $nodeId with broker epoch ${_brokerEpoch}")
   435→          scheduleNextCommunicationImmediately() // Immediately send a heartbeat
   436→        } else {
   437→          info(s"Unable to register broker $nodeId because the controller returned " +
   438→            s"error $errorCode")
   439→          scheduleNextCommunicationAfterFailure()
   440→        }
   441→      }
   442→    }
   443→  }
   444→
   445→  private def sendBrokerHeartbeat(): Unit = {
   446→    val metadataOffset = _highestMetadataOffsetProvider()
   447→    val data = new BrokerHeartbeatRequestData().
   448→      setBrokerEpoch(_brokerEpoch).
   449→      setBrokerId(nodeId).
   450→      setCurrentMetadataOffset(metadataOffset).
   451→      setWantFence(!readyToUnfence).
   452→      setWantShutDown(_state == BrokerState.PENDING_CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN).
   453→      setOfflineLogDirs(offlineDirs.keys.toSeq.asJava)
   454→    if (isTraceEnabled) {
   455→      trace(s"Sending broker heartbeat $data")
   456→    }
   457→    val handler = new BrokerHeartbeatResponseHandler(offlineDirs.keys)
   458→    _channelManager.sendRequest(new BrokerHeartbeatRequest.Builder(data), handler)
   459→    communicationInFlight = true
   460→  }
   461→
   462→  // the response handler is not invoked from the event handler thread,
   463→  // so it is not safe to update state here, instead, schedule an event
   464→  // to continue handling the response on the event handler thread
   465→  private class BrokerHeartbeatResponseHandler(currentOfflineDirs: Iterable[Uuid]) extends ControllerRequestCompletionHandler {
   466→    override def onComplete(response: ClientResponse): Unit = {
   467→      eventQueue.prepend(new BrokerHeartbeatResponseEvent(response, false, currentOfflineDirs))
   468→    }
   469→
   470→    override def onTimeout(): Unit = {
   471→      info("Unable to send a heartbeat because the RPC got timed out before it could be sent.")
   472→      eventQueue.prepend(new BrokerHeartbeatResponseEvent(null, true, currentOfflineDirs))
   473→    }
   474→  }
   475→
   476→  private class BrokerHeartbeatResponseEvent(response: ClientResponse, timedOut: Boolean,
   477→                                             currentOfflineDirs: Iterable[Uuid]) extends EventQueue.Event {
   478→    override def run(): Unit = {
   479→      communicationInFlight = false
   480→      if (timedOut) {
   481→        scheduleNextCommunicationAfterFailure()
   482→        return
   483→      }
   484→      if (response.authenticationException() != null) {
   485→        error(s"Unable to send broker heartbeat for $nodeId because of an " +
   486→          "authentication exception.", response.authenticationException())
   487→        scheduleNextCommunicationAfterFailure()
   488→      } else if (response.versionMismatch() != null) {
   489→        error(s"Unable to send broker heartbeat for $nodeId because of an API " +
   490→          "version problem.", response.versionMismatch())
   491→        scheduleNextCommunicationAfterFailure()
   492→      } else if (response.responseBody() == null) {
   493→        warn(s"Unable to send broker heartbeat for $nodeId. Retrying.")
   494→        scheduleNextCommunicationAfterFailure()
   495→      } else if (!response.responseBody().isInstanceOf[BrokerHeartbeatResponse]) {
   496→        error(s"Unable to send broker heartbeat for $nodeId because the controller " +
   497→          "returned an invalid response type.")
   498→        scheduleNextCommunicationAfterFailure()
   499→      } else {
   500→        val message = response.responseBody().asInstanceOf[BrokerHeartbeatResponse]
   501→        val errorCode = Errors.forCode(message.data().errorCode())
   502→        if (errorCode == Errors.NONE) {
   503→          val responseData = message.data()
   504→          currentOfflineDirs.foreach(cur => offlineDirs.put(cur, true))
   505→          _state match {
   506→            case BrokerState.STARTING =>
   507→              if (responseData.isCaughtUp) {
   508→                info(s"The broker has caught up. Transitioning from STARTING to RECOVERY.")
   509→                _state = BrokerState.RECOVERY
   510→                initialCatchUpFuture.complete(null)
   511→              } else {
   512→                debug(s"The broker is STARTING. Still waiting to catch up with cluster metadata.")
   513→              }
   514→              // Schedule the heartbeat after only 10 ms so that in the case where
   515→              // there is no recovery work to be done, we start up a bit quicker.
   516→              scheduleNextCommunication(NANOSECONDS.convert(10, MILLISECONDS))
   517→            case BrokerState.RECOVERY =>
   518→              if (!responseData.isFenced) {
   519→                info(s"The broker has been unfenced. Transitioning from RECOVERY to RUNNING.")
   520→                initialUnfenceFuture.complete(null)
   521→                _state = BrokerState.RUNNING
   522→              } else {
   523→                info(s"The broker is in RECOVERY.")
   524→              }
   525→              scheduleNextCommunicationAfterSuccess()
   526→            case BrokerState.RUNNING =>
   527→              debug(s"The broker is RUNNING. Processing heartbeat response.")
   528→              scheduleNextCommunicationAfterSuccess()
   529→            case BrokerState.PENDING_CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN =>
   530→              if (!responseData.shouldShutDown()) {
   531→                info(s"The broker is in PENDING_CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN state, still waiting " +
   532→                  "for the active controller.")
   533→                if (!gotControlledShutdownResponse) {
   534→                  // If this is the first pending controlled shutdown response we got,
   535→                  // schedule our next heartbeat a little bit sooner than we usually would.
   536→                  // In the case where controlled shutdown completes quickly, this will
   537→                  // speed things up a little bit.
   538→                  scheduleNextCommunication(NANOSECONDS.convert(50, MILLISECONDS))
   539→                } else {
   540→                  scheduleNextCommunicationAfterSuccess()
   541→                }
   542→              } else {
   543→                info(s"The controller has asked us to exit controlled shutdown.")
   544→                beginShutdown()
   545→              }
   546→              gotControlledShutdownResponse = true
   547→            case BrokerState.SHUTTING_DOWN =>
   548→              info(s"The broker is SHUTTING_DOWN. Ignoring heartbeat response.")
   549→            case _ =>
   550→              error(s"Unexpected broker state ${_state}")
   551→              scheduleNextCommunicationAfterSuccess()
   552→          }
   553→        } else {
   554→          warn(s"Broker $nodeId sent a heartbeat request but received error $errorCode.")
   555→          scheduleNextCommunicationAfterFailure()
   556→        }
   557→      }
   558→    }
   559→  }
   560→
   561→  private def scheduleNextCommunicationImmediately(): Unit = {
   562→      scheduleNextCommunication(0)
   563→  }
   564→
   565→  private def scheduleNextCommunicationAfterFailure(): Unit = {
   566→    nextSchedulingShouldBeImmediate = false // never immediately reschedule after a failure
   567→    scheduleNextCommunication(NANOSECONDS.convert(
   568→      config.brokerHeartbeatIntervalMs.longValue() , MILLISECONDS))
   569→  }
   570→
   571→  private def scheduleNextCommunicationAfterSuccess(): Unit = {
   572→    scheduleNextCommunication(NANOSECONDS.convert(
   573→      config.brokerHeartbeatIntervalMs.longValue() , MILLISECONDS))
   574→  }
   575→
   576→  private def scheduleNextCommunication(intervalNs: Long): Unit = {
   577→    val adjustedIntervalNs = if (nextSchedulingShouldBeImmediate) 0 else intervalNs
   578→    nextSchedulingShouldBeImmediate = false
   579→    trace(s"Scheduling next communication at ${MILLISECONDS.convert(adjustedIntervalNs, NANOSECONDS)} " +
   580→      "ms from now.")
   581→    val deadlineNs = time.nanoseconds() + adjustedIntervalNs
   582→    eventQueue.scheduleDeferred("communication",
   583→      new DeadlineFunction(deadlineNs),
   584→      new CommunicationEvent())
   585→  }
   586→
   587→  private class RegistrationTimeoutEvent extends EventQueue.Event {
   588→    override def run(): Unit = {
   589→      if (!initialRegistrationSucceeded) {
   590→        error("Shutting down because we were unable to register with the controller quorum.")
   591→        eventQueue.beginShutdown("registrationTimeout")
   592→      }
   593→    }
   594→  }
   595→
   596→  private class CommunicationEvent extends EventQueue.Event {
   597→    override def run(): Unit = {
   598→      if (communicationInFlight) {
   599→        trace("Delaying communication because there is already one in flight.")
   600→        nextSchedulingShouldBeImmediate = true
   601→      } else if (registered) {
   602→        sendBrokerHeartbeat()
   603→      } else {
   604→        sendBrokerRegistration()
   605→      }
   606→    }
   607→  }
   608→
   609→  private class ShutdownEvent extends EventQueue.Event {
   610→    override def run(): Unit = {
   611→      info(s"Transitioning from ${_state} to ${BrokerState.SHUTTING_DOWN}.")
   612→      _state = BrokerState.SHUTTING_DOWN
   613→      controlledShutdownFuture.complete(null)
   614→      initialCatchUpFuture.cancel(false)
   615→      initialUnfenceFuture.cancel(false)

   617→        _channelManager.shutdown()
   618→        _channelManager = null
   619→      }
   620→    }
   621→  }
   622→}

    39→/**

    52→ * are placed on the queue to be processed sequentially. As described in the JavaDoc for

    55→ * written from the event queue thread.

   209→    threadNamePrefix + "lifecycle-manager-",

   212→  /**

   214→   *

   253→  }

   259→    }
   260→  }
   261→
   262→  def brokerEpoch: Long = _brokerEpoch
   263→
   264→  def state: BrokerState = _state
   265→
   266→  private class BeginControlledShutdownEvent extends EventQueue.Event {
   267→    override def run(): Unit = {
   268→      _state match {
   269→        case BrokerState.PENDING_CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN =>
   270→          info("Attempted to enter pending controlled shutdown state, but we are " +
   271→          "already in that state.")
   272→        case BrokerState.RUNNING =>
   273→          info("Beginning controlled shutdown.")
   274→          _state = BrokerState.PENDING_CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN
   275→          // Send the next heartbeat immediately in order to let the controller
   276→          // begin processing the controlled shutdown as soon as possible.
   277→          scheduleNextCommunicationImmediately()
   278→
   279→        case _ =>
   280→          info(s"Skipping controlled shutdown because we are in state ${_state}.")
   281→          beginShutdown()
   282→      }
   283→    }
   284→  }
   285→
   286→  /**
   287→   * Enter the controlled shutdown state if we are in RUNNING state.
   288→   * Or, if we're not running, shut down immediately.
   289→   */

   292→  }
   293→
   294→  /**
   295→   * Start shutting down the BrokerLifecycleManager, but do not block.
   296→   */
   297→  def beginShutdown(): Unit = {
   298→    eventQueue.beginShutdown("beginShutdown")
   299→  }
   300→
   301→  /**
   302→   * Shut down the BrokerLifecycleManager and block until all threads are joined.
   303→   */
   304→  def close(): Unit = {
   305→    beginShutdown()
   306→    eventQueue.close()
   307→  }
   308→
   309→  private class SetReadyToUnfenceEvent extends EventQueue.Event {
   310→    override def run(): Unit = {

   315→
   316→  private class OfflineDirEvent(val dir: Uuid) extends EventQueue.Event {
   317→    override def run(): Unit = {

   329→  private class OfflineDirBrokerFailureEvent(offlineDir: Uuid) extends EventQueue.Event {

   338→  private class StartupEvent(highestMetadataOffsetProvider: () => Long,

   360→  private def sendBrokerRegistration(): Unit = {

   462→  // the response handler is not invoked from the event handler thread,

   476→  private class BrokerHeartbeatResponseEvent(response: ClientResponse, timedOut: Boolean,

   487→        scheduleNextCommunicationAfterFailure()
   488→      } else if (response.versionMismatch() != null) {
   489→        error(s"Unable to send broker heartbeat for $nodeId because of an API " +
   490→          "version problem.", response.versionMismatch())
   491→        scheduleNextCommunicationAfterFailure()
   492→      } else if (response.responseBody() == null) {
   493→        warn(s"Unable to send broker heartbeat for $nodeId. Retrying.")
   494→        scheduleNextCommunicationAfterFailure()
   495→      } else if (!response.responseBody().isInstanceOf[BrokerHeartbeatResponse]) {
   496→        error(s"Unable to send broker heartbeat for $nodeId because the controller " +
   497→          "returned an invalid response type.")
   498→        scheduleNextCommunicationAfterFailure()
   499→      } else {
   500→        val message = response.responseBody().asInstanceOf[BrokerHeartbeatResponse]
   501→        val errorCode = Errors.forCode(message.data().errorCode())
   502→        if (errorCode == Errors.NONE) {
   503→          val responseData = message.data()
   504→          currentOfflineDirs.foreach(cur => offlineDirs.put(cur, true))
   505→          _state match {
   506→            case BrokerState.STARTING =>
   507→              if (responseData.isCaughtUp) {
   508→                info(s"The broker has caught up. Transitioning from STARTING to RECOVERY.")
   509→                _state = BrokerState.RECOVERY
   510→                initialCatchUpFuture.complete(null)
   511→              } else {
   512→                debug(s"The broker is STARTING. Still waiting to catch up with cluster metadata.")
   513→              }
   514→              // Schedule the heartbeat after only 10 ms so that in the case where
   515→              // there is no recovery work to be done, we start up a bit quicker.
   516→              scheduleNextCommunication(NANOSECONDS.convert(10, MILLISECONDS))
   517→            case BrokerState.RECOVERY =>
   518→              if (!responseData.isFenced) {
   519→                info(s"The broker has been unfenced. Transitioning from RECOVERY to RUNNING.")
   520→                initialUnfenceFuture.complete(null)
   521→                _state = BrokerState.RUNNING
   522→              } else {
   523→                info(s"The broker is in RECOVERY.")
   524→              }
   525→              scheduleNextCommunicationAfterSuccess()
   526→            case BrokerState.RUNNING =>
   527→              debug(s"The broker is RUNNING. Processing heartbeat response.")
   528→              scheduleNextCommunicationAfterSuccess()
   529→            case BrokerState.PENDING_CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN =>
   530→              if (!responseData.shouldShutDown()) {
   531→                info(s"The broker is in PENDING_CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN state, still waiting " +
   532→                  "for the active controller.")
   533→                if (!gotControlledShutdownResponse) {
   534→                  // If this is the first pending controlled shutdown response we got,
   535→                  // schedule our next heartbeat a little bit sooner than we usually would.
   536→                  // In the case where controlled shutdown completes quickly, this will
   537→                  // speed things up a little bit.
   538→                  scheduleNextCommunication(NANOSECONDS.convert(50, MILLISECONDS))
   539→                } else {
   540→                  scheduleNextCommunicationAfterSuccess()
   541→                }
   542→              } else {
   543→                info(s"The controller has asked us to exit controlled shutdown.")
   544→                beginShutdown()
   545→              }
   546→              gotControlledShutdownResponse = true
   547→            case BrokerState.SHUTTING_DOWN =>
   548→              info(s"The broker is SHUTTING_DOWN. Ignoring heartbeat response.")
   549→            case _ =>
   550→              error(s"Unexpected broker state ${_state}")
   551→              scheduleNextCommunicationAfterSuccess()
   552→          }
   553→        } else {
   554→          warn(s"Broker $nodeId sent a heartbeat request but received error $errorCode.")
   555→          scheduleNextCommunicationAfterFailure()
   556→        }
   557→      }
   558→    }
   559→  }
   560→
   561→  private def scheduleNextCommunicationImmediately(): Unit = {
   562→      scheduleNextCommunication(0)
   563→  }
   564→
   565→  private def scheduleNextCommunicationAfterFailure(): Unit = {
   566→    nextSchedulingShouldBeImmediate = false // never immediately reschedule after a failure
   567→    scheduleNextCommunication(NANOSECONDS.convert(
   568→      config.brokerHeartbeatIntervalMs.longValue() , MILLISECONDS))
   569→  }
   570→
   571→  private def scheduleNextCommunicationAfterSuccess(): Unit = {
   572→    scheduleNextCommunication(NANOSECONDS.convert(
   573→      config.brokerHeartbeatIntervalMs.longValue() , MILLISECONDS))
   574→  }
   575→
   576→  private def scheduleNextCommunication(intervalNs: Long): Unit = {
   577→    val adjustedIntervalNs = if (nextSchedulingShouldBeImmediate) 0 else intervalNs
   578→    nextSchedulingShouldBeImmediate = false
   579→    trace(s"Scheduling next communication at ${MILLISECONDS.convert(adjustedIntervalNs, NANOSECONDS)} " +
   580→      "ms from now.")
   581→    val deadlineNs = time.nanoseconds() + adjustedIntervalNs
   582→    eventQueue.scheduleDeferred("communication",
   583→      new DeadlineFunction(deadlineNs),
   584→      new CommunicationEvent())
   585→  }
   586→
   587→  private class RegistrationTimeoutEvent extends EventQueue.Event {
   588→    override def run(): Unit = {
   589→      if (!initialRegistrationSucceeded) {
   590→        error("Shutting down because we were unable to register with the controller quorum.")
   591→        eventQueue.beginShutdown("registrationTimeout")
   592→      }
   593→    }
   594→  }
   595→
   596→  private class CommunicationEvent extends EventQueue.Event {
   597→    override def run(): Unit = {
   598→      if (communicationInFlight) {
   599→        trace("Delaying communication because there is already one in flight.")
   600→        nextSchedulingShouldBeImmediate = true
   601→      } else if (registered) {
   602→        sendBrokerHeartbeat()
   603→      } else {
   604→        sendBrokerRegistration()
   605→      }
   606→    }
   607→  }
   608→
   609→  private class ShutdownEvent extends EventQueue.Event {
   610→    override def run(): Unit = {
   611→      info(s"Transitioning from ${_state} to ${BrokerState.SHUTTING_DOWN}.")
   612→      _state = BrokerState.SHUTTING_DOWN
   613→      controlledShutdownFuture.complete(null)
   614→      initialCatchUpFuture.cancel(false)
   615→      initialUnfenceFuture.cancel(false)
   616→      if (_channelManager != null) {
   617→        _channelManager.shutdown()
   618→        _channelManager = null
   619→      }
   620→    }
   621→  }
   622→}
   623→
