    17→package kafka.server
    18→
    19→import java.util
    20→import java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit.{MILLISECONDS, NANOSECONDS}
    21→import java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture
    22→import kafka.utils.Logging
    23→import org.apache.kafka.clients.ClientResponse
    24→import org.apache.kafka.common.Uuid
    25→import org.apache.kafka.common.message.BrokerRegistrationRequestData.ListenerCollection
    26→import org.apache.kafka.common.message.{BrokerHeartbeatRequestData, BrokerRegistrationRequestData}
    27→import org.apache.kafka.common.protocol.Errors
    28→import org.apache.kafka.common.requests.{BrokerHeartbeatRequest, BrokerHeartbeatResponse, BrokerRegistrationRequest, BrokerRegistrationResponse}
    29→import org.apache.kafka.metadata.{BrokerState, VersionRange}
    30→import org.apache.kafka.queue.EventQueue.DeadlineFunction
    31→import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.{LogContext, Time}
    32→import org.apache.kafka.queue.{EventQueue, KafkaEventQueue}
    33→import org.apache.kafka.server.common.{ControllerRequestCompletionHandler, NodeToControllerChannelManager}
    34→
    35→import java.util.{Comparator, OptionalLong}
    36→import scala.collection.mutable
    37→import scala.jdk.CollectionConverters._
    38→
    39→/**
    40→ * The broker lifecycle manager owns the broker state.
    41→ *
    42→ * Its inputs are messages passed in from other parts of the broker and from the
    43→ * controller: requests to start up, or shut down, for example. Its output are the broker
    44→ * state and various futures that can be used to wait for broker state transitions to
    45→ * occur.
    46→ *
    47→ * The lifecycle manager handles registering the broker with the controller, as described
    48→ * in KIP-631. After registration is complete, it handles sending periodic broker
    49→ * heartbeats and processing the responses.
    50→ *
    51→ * This code uses an event queue paradigm. Modifications get translated into events, which
    52→ * are placed on the queue to be processed sequentially. As described in the JavaDoc for
    53→ * each variable, most mutable state can be accessed only from that event queue thread.
    54→ * In some cases we expose a volatile variable which can be read from any thread, but only
    55→ * written from the event queue thread.
    56→ */
    57→class BrokerLifecycleManager(
    58→  val config: KafkaConfig,
    59→  val time: Time,
    60→  val threadNamePrefix: String,
    61→  val logDirs: Set[Uuid],
    62→  val shutdownHook: () => Unit = () => {}
    63→) extends Logging {
    64→
    65→  private def logPrefix(): String = {
    66→    val builder = new StringBuilder("[BrokerLifecycleManager")
    67→    builder.append(" id=").append(config.nodeId)
    68→    builder.append("] ")
    69→    builder.toString()
    70→  }
    71→
    72→  val logContext = new LogContext(logPrefix())
    73→
    74→  this.logIdent = logContext.logPrefix()
    75→
    76→  /**
    77→   * The broker id.
    78→   */
    79→  private val nodeId = config.nodeId
    80→
    81→  /**
    82→   * The broker rack, or null if there is no configured rack.
    83→   */
    84→  private val rack = config.rack
    85→
    86→  /**
    87→   * How long to wait for registration to succeed before failing the startup process.
    88→   */
    89→  private val initialTimeoutNs =
    90→    MILLISECONDS.toNanos(config.initialRegistrationTimeoutMs.longValue())
    91→
    92→  /**
    93→   * The broker incarnation ID.  This ID uniquely identifies each time we start the broker
    94→   */
    95→  val incarnationId: Uuid = Uuid.randomUuid()
    96→
    97→  /**
    98→   * A future which is completed just as soon as the broker has caught up with the latest
    99→   * metadata offset for the first time.
   100→   */
   101→  val initialCatchUpFuture: CompletableFuture[Void] = new CompletableFuture[Void]()
   102→
   103→  /**
   104→   * A future which is completed when the broker is unfenced for the first time.
   105→   */
   106→  val initialUnfenceFuture: CompletableFuture[Void] = new CompletableFuture[Void]()
   107→
   108→  /**
   109→   * A future which is completed when controlled shutdown is done.
   110→   */
   111→  val controlledShutdownFuture: CompletableFuture[Void] = new CompletableFuture[Void]()
   112→
   113→  /**
   114→   * The broker epoch, or -1 if the broker has not yet registered.  This variable can only
   115→   * be written from the event queue thread.
   116→   */
   117→  @volatile private var _brokerEpoch = -1L
   118→
   119→  /**
   120→   * The current broker state.  This variable can only be written from the event queue
   121→   * thread.
   122→   */
   123→  @volatile private var _state = BrokerState.NOT_RUNNING
   124→
   125→  /**
   126→   * A thread-safe callback function which gives this manager the current highest metadata
   127→   * offset.  This variable can only be read or written from the event queue thread.
   128→   */
   129→  private var _highestMetadataOffsetProvider: () => Long = _
   130→
   131→  /**
   132→   * True only if we are ready to unfence the broker.  This variable can only be read or
   133→   * written from the event queue thread.
   134→   */
   135→  private var readyToUnfence = false
   136→
   137→  /**
   138→   * Map of accumulated offline directories. The value is true if the directory couldn't be communicated
   139→   * to the Controller.
   140→   * This variable can only be read or written from the event queue thread.
   141→   */
   142→  private var offlineDirs = mutable.Map[Uuid, Boolean]()
   143→
   144→  /**
   145→   * True if we sent an event queue to the active controller requesting controlled
   146→   * shutdown.  This variable can only be read or written from the event queue thread.
   147→   */
   148→  private var gotControlledShutdownResponse = false
   149→
   150→  /**
   151→   * Whether or not this broker is registered with the controller quorum.
   152→   * This variable can only be read or written from the event queue thread.
   153→   */
   154→  private var registered = false
   155→
   156→  /**
   157→   * True if a request has been sent and a response or timeout has not yet been processed.
   158→   * This variable can only be read or written from the event queue thread.
   159→   */
   160→  private var communicationInFlight = false
   161→
   162→  /**
   163→   * True if we should schedule the next communication immediately. This is used to delay
   164→   * an immediate scheduling of a communication event if one is already in flight.
   165→   * This variable can only be read or written from the event queue thread.
   166→   */
   167→  private var nextSchedulingShouldBeImmediate = false
   168→
   169→  /**
   170→   * True if the initial registration succeeded.  This variable can only be read or
   171→   * written from the event queue thread.
   172→   */
   173→  private var initialRegistrationSucceeded = false
   174→
   175→  /**
   176→   * The cluster ID, or null if this manager has not been started yet.  This variable can
   177→   * only be read or written from the event queue thread.
   178→   */
   179→  private var _clusterId: String = _
   180→
   181→  /**
   182→   * The listeners which this broker advertises.  This variable can only be read or
   183→   * written from the event queue thread.
   184→   */
   185→  private var _advertisedListeners: ListenerCollection = _
   186→
   187→  /**
   188→   * The features supported by this broker.  This variable can only be read or written
   189→   * from the event queue thread.
   190→   */
   191→  private var _supportedFeatures: util.Map[String, VersionRange] = _
   192→
   193→  /**
   194→   * The channel manager, or null if this manager has not been started yet.  This variable
   195→   * can only be read or written from the event queue thread.
   196→   */
   197→  private var _channelManager: NodeToControllerChannelManager = _
   198→
   199→  /**
   200→   * The broker epoch from the previous run, or empty if the epoch is not found.
   201→   */
   202→  @volatile private var previousBrokerEpoch: OptionalLong = OptionalLong.empty()
   203→
   204→  /**
   205→   * The event queue.
   206→   */
   207→  private[server] val eventQueue = new KafkaEventQueue(time,
   208→    logContext,
   209→    threadNamePrefix + "lifecycle-manager-",
   210→    new ShutdownEvent())
   211→
   212→  /**
   213→   * Start the BrokerLifecycleManager.
   214→   *
   215→   * @param highestMetadataOffsetProvider Provides the current highest metadata offset.
   216→   * @param channelManager                The NodeToControllerChannelManager to use.
   217→   * @param clusterId                     The cluster ID.
   218→   * @param advertisedListeners           The advertised listeners for this broker.
   219→   * @param supportedFeatures             The features for this broker.
   220→   * @param previousBrokerEpoch           The broker epoch before the reboot.
   221→   *
   222→   */
   223→  def start(highestMetadataOffsetProvider: () => Long,
   224→            channelManager: NodeToControllerChannelManager,
   225→            clusterId: String,
   226→            advertisedListeners: ListenerCollection,
   227→            supportedFeatures: util.Map[String, VersionRange],
   228→            previousBrokerEpoch: OptionalLong): Unit = {
   229→    this.previousBrokerEpoch = previousBrokerEpoch
   230→    eventQueue.append(new StartupEvent(highestMetadataOffsetProvider,
   231→      channelManager, clusterId, advertisedListeners, supportedFeatures))
   232→  }
   233→
   234→  def setReadyToUnfence(): CompletableFuture[Void] = {
   235→    eventQueue.append(new SetReadyToUnfenceEvent())
   236→    initialUnfenceFuture
   237→  }
   238→
   239→  /**
   240→   * Propagate directory failures to the controller.
   241→   * @param directory The ID for the directory that failed.
   242→   */
   243→  def propagateDirectoryFailure(directory: Uuid, timeout: Long): Unit = {
   244→    eventQueue.append(new OfflineDirEvent(directory))
   245→    // If we can't communicate the offline directory to the controller, we should shut down.
   246→    eventQueue.scheduleDeferred("offlineDirFailure",
   247→      new DeadlineFunction(time.nanoseconds() + MILLISECONDS.toNanos(timeout)),
   248→      new OfflineDirBrokerFailureEvent(directory))
   249→  }
   250→
   251→  def resendBrokerRegistration(): Unit = {
   252→    eventQueue.append(new ResendBrokerRegistrationEvent())
   253→  }
   254→
   255→  private class ResendBrokerRegistrationEvent extends EventQueue.Event {
   256→    override def run(): Unit = {
   257→      registered = false
   258→      scheduleNextCommunicationImmediately()
   259→    }
   260→  }
   261→
   262→  def brokerEpoch: Long = _brokerEpoch
   263→
   264→  def state: BrokerState = _state
   265→
   266→  private class BeginControlledShutdownEvent extends EventQueue.Event {
   267→    override def run(): Unit = {
   268→      _state match {
   269→        case BrokerState.PENDING_CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN =>
   270→          info("Attempted to enter pending controlled shutdown state, but we are " +
   271→          "already in that state.")
   272→        case BrokerState.RUNNING =>
   273→          info("Beginning controlled shutdown.")
   274→          _state = BrokerState.PENDING_CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN
   275→          // Send the next heartbeat immediately in order to let the controller
   276→          // begin processing the controlled shutdown as soon as possible.
   277→          scheduleNextCommunicationImmediately()
   278→
   279→        case _ =>
   280→          info(s"Skipping controlled shutdown because we are in state ${_state}.")
   281→          beginShutdown()
   282→      }
   283→    }
   284→  }
   285→
   286→  /**
   287→   * Enter the controlled shutdown state if we are in RUNNING state.
   288→   * Or, if we're not running, shut down immediately.
   289→   */
   290→  def beginControlledShutdown(): Unit = {
   291→    eventQueue.append(new BeginControlledShutdownEvent())
   292→  }
   293→
   294→  /**
   295→   * Start shutting down the BrokerLifecycleManager, but do not block.
   296→   */
   297→  def beginShutdown(): Unit = {
   298→    eventQueue.beginShutdown("beginShutdown")
   299→  }
   300→
   301→  /**
   302→   * Shut down the BrokerLifecycleManager and block until all threads are joined.
   303→   */
   304→  def close(): Unit = {
   305→    beginShutdown()
   306→    eventQueue.close()
   307→  }
   308→
   309→  private class SetReadyToUnfenceEvent extends EventQueue.Event {
   310→    override def run(): Unit = {
   311→      readyToUnfence = true
   312→      scheduleNextCommunicationImmediately()
   313→    }
   314→  }
   315→
   316→  private class OfflineDirEvent(val dir: Uuid) extends EventQueue.Event {
   317→    override def run(): Unit = {
   318→      if (offlineDirs.isEmpty) {
   319→        offlineDirs = mutable.Map(dir -> false)
   320→      } else {
   321→        offlineDirs += (dir -> false)
   322→      }
   323→      if (registered) {
   324→        scheduleNextCommunicationImmediately()
   325→      }
   326→    }
   327→  }
   328→
   329→  private class OfflineDirBrokerFailureEvent(offlineDir: Uuid) extends EventQueue.Event {
   330→    override def run(): Unit = {
   331→      if (!offlineDirs.getOrElse(offlineDir, false)) {
   332→        error(s"Shutting down because couldn't communicate offline log dir $offlineDir with controllers")
   333→        shutdownHook()
   334→      }
   335→    }
   336→  }
   337→
   338→  private class StartupEvent(highestMetadataOffsetProvider: () => Long,
   339→                     channelManager: NodeToControllerChannelManager,
   340→                     clusterId: String,
   341→                     advertisedListeners: ListenerCollection,
   342→                     supportedFeatures: util.Map[String, VersionRange]) extends EventQueue.Event {
   343→    override def run(): Unit = {
   344→      _highestMetadataOffsetProvider = highestMetadataOffsetProvider
   345→      _channelManager = channelManager
   346→      _channelManager.start()
   347→      _state = BrokerState.STARTING
   348→      _clusterId = clusterId
   349→      _advertisedListeners = advertisedListeners.duplicate()
   350→      _supportedFeatures = new util.HashMap[String, VersionRange](supportedFeatures)
   351→      eventQueue.scheduleDeferred("initialRegistrationTimeout",
   352→        new DeadlineFunction(time.nanoseconds() + initialTimeoutNs),
   353→        new RegistrationTimeoutEvent())
   354→      sendBrokerRegistration()
   355→      info(s"Incarnation $incarnationId of broker $nodeId in cluster $clusterId " +
   356→        "is now STARTING.")
   357→    }
   358→  }
   359→
   360→  private def sendBrokerRegistration(): Unit = {
   361→    val features = new BrokerRegistrationRequestData.FeatureCollection()
   362→    _supportedFeatures.asScala.foreach {
   363→      case (name, range) => features.add(new BrokerRegistrationRequestData.Feature().
   364→        setName(name).
   365→        setMinSupportedVersion(range.min()).
   366→        setMaxSupportedVersion(range.max()))
   367→    }
   368→    val sortedLogDirs = new util.ArrayList[Uuid]
   369→    logDirs.foreach(sortedLogDirs.add)
   370→    sortedLogDirs.sort(new Comparator[Uuid]() {
   371→      override def compare(a: Uuid, b: Uuid): Int = a.compareTo(b)
   372→    })
   373→    val data = new BrokerRegistrationRequestData().
   374→        setBrokerId(nodeId).
   375→        setIsMigratingZkBroker(false).
   376→        setClusterId(_clusterId).
   377→        setFeatures(features).
   378→        setIncarnationId(incarnationId).
   379→        setListeners(_advertisedListeners).
   380→        setRack(rack.orNull).
   381→        setPreviousBrokerEpoch(previousBrokerEpoch.orElse(-1L)).
   382→        setLogDirs(sortedLogDirs)
   383→    if (isDebugEnabled) {
   384→      debug(s"Sending broker registration $data")
   385→    }
   386→    _channelManager.sendRequest(new BrokerRegistrationRequest.Builder(data),
   387→      new BrokerRegistrationResponseHandler())
   388→    communicationInFlight = true
   389→  }
   390→
   391→  // the response handler is not invoked from the event handler thread,
   392→  // so it is not safe to update state here, instead, schedule an event
   393→  // to continue handling the response on the event handler thread
   394→  private class BrokerRegistrationResponseHandler extends ControllerRequestCompletionHandler {
   395→    override def onComplete(response: ClientResponse): Unit = {
   396→      eventQueue.prepend(new BrokerRegistrationResponseEvent(response, false))
   397→    }
   398→
   399→    override def onTimeout(): Unit = {
   400→      info(s"Unable to register the broker because the RPC got timed out before it could be sent.")
   401→      eventQueue.prepend(new BrokerRegistrationResponseEvent(null, true))
   402→    }
   403→  }
   404→
   405→  private class BrokerRegistrationResponseEvent(response: ClientResponse, timedOut: Boolean) extends EventQueue.Event {
   406→    override def run(): Unit = {
   407→      communicationInFlight = false
   408→      if (timedOut) {
   409→        scheduleNextCommunicationAfterFailure()
   410→        return
   411→      }
   412→      if (response.authenticationException() != null) {
   413→        error(s"Unable to register broker $nodeId because of an authentication exception.",
   414→          response.authenticationException())
   415→        scheduleNextCommunicationAfterFailure()
   416→      } else if (response.versionMismatch() != null) {
   417→        error(s"Unable to register broker $nodeId because of an API version problem.",
   418→          response.versionMismatch())
   419→        scheduleNextCommunicationAfterFailure()
   420→      } else if (response.responseBody() == null) {
   421→        warn(s"Unable to register broker $nodeId.")
   422→        scheduleNextCommunicationAfterFailure()
   423→      } else if (!response.responseBody().isInstanceOf[BrokerRegistrationResponse]) {
   424→        error(s"Unable to register broker $nodeId because the controller returned an " +
   425→          "invalid response type.")
   426→        scheduleNextCommunicationAfterFailure()
   427→      } else {
   428→        val message = response.responseBody().asInstanceOf[BrokerRegistrationResponse]
   429→        val errorCode = Errors.forCode(message.data().errorCode())
   430→        if (errorCode == Errors.NONE) {
   431→          _brokerEpoch = message.data().brokerEpoch()
   432→          registered = true
   433→          initialRegistrationSucceeded = true
   434→          info(s"Successfully registered broker $nodeId with broker epoch ${_brokerEpoch}")
   435→          scheduleNextCommunicationImmediately() // Immediately send a heartbeat
   436→        } else {
   437→          info(s"Unable to register broker $nodeId because the controller returned " +
   438→            s"error $errorCode")
   439→          scheduleNextCommunicationAfterFailure()
   440→        }
   441→      }
   442→    }
   443→  }
   444→
   445→  private def sendBrokerHeartbeat(): Unit = {
   446→    val metadataOffset = _highestMetadataOffsetProvider()
   447→    val data = new BrokerHeartbeatRequestData().
   448→      setBrokerEpoch(_brokerEpoch).
   449→      setBrokerId(nodeId).
   450→      setCurrentMetadataOffset(metadataOffset).
   451→      setWantFence(!readyToUnfence).
   452→      setWantShutDown(_state == BrokerState.PENDING_CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN).
   453→      setOfflineLogDirs(offlineDirs.keys.toSeq.asJava)
   454→    if (isTraceEnabled) {
   455→      trace(s"Sending broker heartbeat $data")
   456→    }
   457→    val handler = new BrokerHeartbeatResponseHandler(offlineDirs.keys)
   458→    _channelManager.sendRequest(new BrokerHeartbeatRequest.Builder(data), handler)
   459→    communicationInFlight = true
   460→  }
   461→
   462→  // the response handler is not invoked from the event handler thread,
   463→  // so it is not safe to update state here, instead, schedule an event
   464→  // to continue handling the response on the event handler thread
   465→  private class BrokerHeartbeatResponseHandler(currentOfflineDirs: Iterable[Uuid]) extends ControllerRequestCompletionHandler {
   466→    override def onComplete(response: ClientResponse): Unit = {
   467→      eventQueue.prepend(new BrokerHeartbeatResponseEvent(response, false, currentOfflineDirs))
   468→    }
   469→
   470→    override def onTimeout(): Unit = {
   471→      info("Unable to send a heartbeat because the RPC got timed out before it could be sent.")
   472→      eventQueue.prepend(new BrokerHeartbeatResponseEvent(null, true, currentOfflineDirs))
   473→    }
   474→  }
   475→
   476→  private class BrokerHeartbeatResponseEvent(response: ClientResponse, timedOut: Boolean,
   477→                                             currentOfflineDirs: Iterable[Uuid]) extends EventQueue.Event {
   478→    override def run(): Unit = {
   479→      communicationInFlight = false
   480→      if (timedOut) {
   481→        scheduleNextCommunicationAfterFailure()
   482→        return
   483→      }
   484→      if (response.authenticationException() != null) {
   485→        error(s"Unable to send broker heartbeat for $nodeId because of an " +
   486→          "authentication exception.", response.authenticationException())
   487→        scheduleNextCommunicationAfterFailure()
   488→      } else if (response.versionMismatch() != null) {
   489→        error(s"Unable to send broker heartbeat for $nodeId because of an API " +
   490→          "version problem.", response.versionMismatch())
   491→        scheduleNextCommunicationAfterFailure()
   492→      } else if (response.responseBody() == null) {
   493→        warn(s"Unable to send broker heartbeat for $nodeId. Retrying.")
   494→        scheduleNextCommunicationAfterFailure()
   495→      } else if (!response.responseBody().isInstanceOf[BrokerHeartbeatResponse]) {
   496→        error(s"Unable to send broker heartbeat for $nodeId because the controller " +
   497→          "returned an invalid response type.")
   498→        scheduleNextCommunicationAfterFailure()
   499→      } else {
   500→        val message = response.responseBody().asInstanceOf[BrokerHeartbeatResponse]
   501→        val errorCode = Errors.forCode(message.data().errorCode())
   502→        if (errorCode == Errors.NONE) {
   503→          val responseData = message.data()
   504→          currentOfflineDirs.foreach(cur => offlineDirs.put(cur, true))
   505→          _state match {
   506→            case BrokerState.STARTING =>
   507→              if (responseData.isCaughtUp) {
   508→                info(s"The broker has caught up. Transitioning from STARTING to RECOVERY.")
   509→                _state = BrokerState.RECOVERY
   510→                initialCatchUpFuture.complete(null)
   511→              } else {
   512→                debug(s"The broker is STARTING. Still waiting to catch up with cluster metadata.")
   513→              }
   514→              // Schedule the heartbeat after only 10 ms so that in the case where
   515→              // there is no recovery work to be done, we start up a bit quicker.
   516→              scheduleNextCommunication(NANOSECONDS.convert(10, MILLISECONDS))
   517→            case BrokerState.RECOVERY =>
   518→              if (!responseData.isFenced) {
   519→                info(s"The broker has been unfenced. Transitioning from RECOVERY to RUNNING.")
   520→                initialUnfenceFuture.complete(null)
   521→                _state = BrokerState.RUNNING
   522→              } else {
   523→                info(s"The broker is in RECOVERY.")
   524→              }
   525→              scheduleNextCommunicationAfterSuccess()
   526→            case BrokerState.RUNNING =>
   527→              debug(s"The broker is RUNNING. Processing heartbeat response.")
   528→              scheduleNextCommunicationAfterSuccess()
   529→            case BrokerState.PENDING_CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN =>
   530→              if (!responseData.shouldShutDown()) {
   531→                info(s"The broker is in PENDING_CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN state, still waiting " +
   532→                  "for the active controller.")
   533→                if (!gotControlledShutdownResponse) {
   534→                  // If this is the first pending controlled shutdown response we got,
   535→                  // schedule our next heartbeat a little bit sooner than we usually would.
   536→                  // In the case where controlled shutdown completes quickly, this will
   537→                  // speed things up a little bit.
   538→                  scheduleNextCommunication(NANOSECONDS.convert(50, MILLISECONDS))
   539→                } else {
   540→                  scheduleNextCommunicationAfterSuccess()
   541→                }
   542→              } else {
   543→                info(s"The controller has asked us to exit controlled shutdown.")
   544→                beginShutdown()
   545→              }
   546→              gotControlledShutdownResponse = true
   547→            case BrokerState.SHUTTING_DOWN =>
   548→              info(s"The broker is SHUTTING_DOWN. Ignoring heartbeat response.")
   549→            case _ =>
   550→              error(s"Unexpected broker state ${_state}")
   551→              scheduleNextCommunicationAfterSuccess()
   552→          }
   553→        } else {
   554→          warn(s"Broker $nodeId sent a heartbeat request but received error $errorCode.")
   555→          scheduleNextCommunicationAfterFailure()
   556→        }
   557→      }
   558→    }
   559→  }
   560→
   561→  private def scheduleNextCommunicationImmediately(): Unit = {
   562→      scheduleNextCommunication(0)
   563→  }
   564→
   565→  private def scheduleNextCommunicationAfterFailure(): Unit = {
   566→    nextSchedulingShouldBeImmediate = false // never immediately reschedule after a failure
   567→    scheduleNextCommunication(NANOSECONDS.convert(
   568→      config.brokerHeartbeatIntervalMs.longValue() , MILLISECONDS))
   569→  }
   570→
   571→  private def scheduleNextCommunicationAfterSuccess(): Unit = {
   572→    scheduleNextCommunication(NANOSECONDS.convert(
   573→      config.brokerHeartbeatIntervalMs.longValue() , MILLISECONDS))
   574→  }
   575→
   576→  private def scheduleNextCommunication(intervalNs: Long): Unit = {
   577→    val adjustedIntervalNs = if (nextSchedulingShouldBeImmediate) 0 else intervalNs
   578→    nextSchedulingShouldBeImmediate = false
   579→    trace(s"Scheduling next communication at ${MILLISECONDS.convert(adjustedIntervalNs, NANOSECONDS)} " +
   580→      "ms from now.")
   581→    val deadlineNs = time.nanoseconds() + adjustedIntervalNs
   582→    eventQueue.scheduleDeferred("communication",
   583→      new DeadlineFunction(deadlineNs),
   584→      new CommunicationEvent())
   585→  }
   586→
   587→  private class RegistrationTimeoutEvent extends EventQueue.Event {
   588→    override def run(): Unit = {
   589→      if (!initialRegistrationSucceeded) {
   590→        error("Shutting down because we were unable to register with the controller quorum.")
   591→        eventQueue.beginShutdown("registrationTimeout")
   592→      }
   593→    }
   594→  }
   595→
   596→  private class CommunicationEvent extends EventQueue.Event {
   597→    override def run(): Unit = {
   598→      if (communicationInFlight) {
   599→        trace("Delaying communication because there is already one in flight.")
   600→        nextSchedulingShouldBeImmediate = true
   601→      } else if (registered) {
   602→        sendBrokerHeartbeat()
   603→      } else {
   604→        sendBrokerRegistration()
   605→      }
   606→    }
   607→  }
   608→
   609→  private class ShutdownEvent extends EventQueue.Event {
   610→    override def run(): Unit = {
   611→      info(s"Transitioning from ${_state} to ${BrokerState.SHUTTING_DOWN}.")
   612→      _state = BrokerState.SHUTTING_DOWN
   613→      controlledShutdownFuture.complete(null)
   614→      initialCatchUpFuture.cancel(false)
   615→      initialUnfenceFuture.cancel(false)
   616→      if (_channelManager != null) {
   617→        _channelManager.shutdown()
   618→        _channelManager = null
   619→      }
   620→    }
   621→  }
   622→}
   623→

1→/*
     2→ * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
     3→ * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with
     4→ * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.

     7→ * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at
     8→ *
     9→ *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
    10→ *
    11→ * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
    12→ * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    13→ * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    14→ * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    15→ * limitations under the License.
    16→ */
    17→package kafka.server
    18→
    19→import java.io.File
    20→import java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture
    21→import kafka.utils.{CoreUtils, Logging, Mx4jLoader}
    22→import org.apache.kafka.common.config.{ConfigDef, ConfigResource}
    23→import org.apache.kafka.common.internals.Topic
    24→import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.{AppInfoParser, Time}
    25→import org.apache.kafka.common.{KafkaException, Uuid}
    26→import org.apache.kafka.metadata.KafkaConfigSchema
    27→import org.apache.kafka.metadata.bootstrap.{BootstrapDirectory, BootstrapMetadata}
    28→import org.apache.kafka.metadata.properties.MetaPropertiesEnsemble.VerificationFlag.{REQUIRE_AT_LEAST_ONE_VALID, REQUIRE_METADATA_LOG_DIR}
    29→import org.apache.kafka.metadata.properties.{MetaProperties, MetaPropertiesEnsemble}
    30→import org.apache.kafka.raft.QuorumConfig
    31→import org.apache.kafka.server.{ProcessRole, ServerSocketFactory}
    32→import org.apache.kafka.server.config.ServerTopicConfigSynonyms
    33→import org.apache.kafka.storage.internals.log.{LogConfig, UnifiedLog}
    34→import org.slf4j.Logger
    35→
    36→import java.util
    37→import java.util.{Optional, OptionalInt}
    38→import scala.jdk.CollectionConverters._
    39→
    40→/**
    41→ * This class implements the KRaft (Kafka Raft) mode server which relies
    42→ * on a KRaft quorum for maintaining cluster metadata. It is responsible for
    43→ * constructing the controller and/or broker based on the `process.roles`
    44→ * configuration and for managing their basic lifecycle (startup and shutdown).
    45→ *
    46→ */
    47→class KafkaRaftServer(
    48→  config: KafkaConfig,
    49→  time: Time,
    50→) extends Server with Logging {
    51→
    52→  this.logIdent = s"[KafkaRaftServer nodeId=${config.nodeId}] "
    53→
    54→  private val (metaPropsEnsemble, bootstrapMetadata) =
    55→    KafkaRaftServer.initializeLogDirs(config, this.logger.underlying, this.logIdent)
    56→
    57→  private val metrics = Server.initializeMetrics(
    58→    config,
    59→    time,
    60→    metaPropsEnsemble.clusterId().get()
    61→  )
    62→
    63→  private val sharedServer = new SharedServer(
    64→    config,
    65→    metaPropsEnsemble,
    66→    time,
    67→    metrics,
    68→    CompletableFuture.completedFuture(QuorumConfig.parseVoterConnections(config.quorumConfig.voters)),
    69→    QuorumConfig.parseBootstrapServers(config.quorumConfig.bootstrapServers),
    70→    new StandardFaultHandlerFactory(),
    71→    ServerSocketFactory.INSTANCE,
    72→  )
    73→
    74→  private val broker: Option[BrokerServer] = if (config.processRoles.contains(ProcessRole.BrokerRole)) {
    75→    Some(new BrokerServer(sharedServer))
    76→  } else {
    77→    None
    78→  }
    79→
    80→  private val controller: Option[ControllerServer] = if (config.processRoles.contains(ProcessRole.ControllerRole)) {
    81→    Some(new ControllerServer(
    82→      sharedServer,
    83→      KafkaRaftServer.configSchema,
    84→      bootstrapMetadata,
    85→    ))
    86→  } else {
    87→    None
    88→  }
    89→
    90→  override def startup(): Unit = {
    91→    Mx4jLoader.maybeLoad()
    92→    // Controller component must be started before the broker component so that
    93→    // the controller endpoints are passed to the KRaft manager
    94→    controller.foreach(_.startup())
    95→    broker.foreach(_.startup())
    96→    AppInfoParser.registerAppInfo(Server.MetricsPrefix, config.brokerId.toString, metrics, time.milliseconds())
    97→    info(KafkaBroker.STARTED_MESSAGE)
    98→  }
    99→
   100→  override def shutdown(): Unit = {
   101→    // In combined mode, we want to shut down the broker first, since the controller may be
   102→    // needed for controlled shutdown. Additionally, the controller shutdown process currently
   103→    // stops the raft client early on, which would disrupt broker shutdown.
   104→    broker.foreach(_.shutdown())
   105→    controller.foreach(_.shutdown())
   106→    CoreUtils.swallow(AppInfoParser.unregisterAppInfo(Server.MetricsPrefix, config.brokerId.toString, metrics), this)
   107→  }
   108→
   109→  override def awaitShutdown(): Unit = {
   110→    broker.foreach(_.awaitShutdown())
   111→    controller.foreach(_.awaitShutdown())
   112→  }
   113→}
   114→
   115→object KafkaRaftServer {
   116→  val MetadataTopic = Topic.CLUSTER_METADATA_TOPIC_NAME
   117→  val MetadataPartition = Topic.CLUSTER_METADATA_TOPIC_PARTITION
   118→  val MetadataTopicId = Uuid.METADATA_TOPIC_ID
   119→
   120→  /**
   121→   * Initialize the configured log directories, including both [[KRaftConfigs.MetadataLogDirProp]]
   122→   * and [[KafkaConfig.LOG_DIR_PROP]]. This method performs basic validation to ensure that all
   123→   * directories are accessible and have been initialized with consistent `meta.properties`.
   124→   *
   125→   * @param config The process configuration
   126→   * @return A tuple containing the loaded meta properties (which are guaranteed to
   127→   *         be consistent across all log dirs) and the offline directories
   128→   */
   129→  def initializeLogDirs(
   130→    config: KafkaConfig,
   131→    log: Logger,
   132→    logPrefix: String
   133→  ): (MetaPropertiesEnsemble, BootstrapMetadata) = {
   134→    // Load and verify the original ensemble.
   135→    val loader = new MetaPropertiesEnsemble.Loader()
   136→    loader.addMetadataLogDir(config.metadataLogDir)
   137→          .addLogDirs(config.logDirs)
   138→    val initialMetaPropsEnsemble = loader.load()
   139→    val verificationFlags = util.EnumSet.of(REQUIRE_AT_LEAST_ONE_VALID, REQUIRE_METADATA_LOG_DIR)
   140→    initialMetaPropsEnsemble.verify(Optional.empty(), OptionalInt.of(config.nodeId), verificationFlags)
   141→
   142→    // Check that the __cluster_metadata-0 topic does not appear outside the metadata directory.
   143→    val metadataPartitionDirName = UnifiedLog.logDirName(MetadataPartition)
   144→    initialMetaPropsEnsemble.logDirProps().keySet().forEach(logDir => {
   145→      if (!logDir.equals(config.metadataLogDir)) {
   146→        val clusterMetadataTopic = new File(logDir, metadataPartitionDirName)
   147→        if (clusterMetadataTopic.exists) {
   148→          throw new KafkaException(s"Found unexpected metadata location in data directory `$clusterMetadataTopic` " +
   149→            s"(the configured metadata directory is ${config.metadataLogDir}).")
   150→        }
   151→      }
   152→    })
   153→
   154→    // Set directory IDs on all directories. Rewrite the files if needed.
   155→    val metaPropsEnsemble = {
   156→      val copier = new MetaPropertiesEnsemble.Copier(initialMetaPropsEnsemble)
   157→      initialMetaPropsEnsemble.nonFailedDirectoryProps().forEachRemaining(e => {
   158→        val logDir = e.getKey
   159→        val metaProps = e.getValue
   160→        if (!metaProps.isPresent()) {
   161→          throw new RuntimeException(s"No `meta.properties` found in $logDir (have you run `kafka-storage.sh` " +
   162→            "to format the directory?)")
   163→        }
   164→        if (!metaProps.get().nodeId().isPresent()) {
   165→          throw new RuntimeException(s"Error: node ID not found in $logDir")
   166→        }
   167→        if (!metaProps.get().clusterId().isPresent()) {
   168→          throw new RuntimeException(s"Error: cluster ID not found in $logDir")
   169→        }
   170→        val builder = new MetaProperties.Builder(metaProps.get())
   171→        if (!builder.directoryId().isPresent()) {
   172→          builder.setDirectoryId(copier.generateValidDirectoryId())
   173→        }
   174→        copier.setLogDirProps(logDir, builder.build())
   175→        copier.setPreWriteHandler((logDir, _, _) => {
   176→          log.info("{}Rewriting {}{}meta.properties", logPrefix, logDir, File.separator)
   177→        })
   178→      })
   179→      copier.writeLogDirChanges()
   180→      copier.copy()
   181→    }
   182→
   183→    // Load the BootstrapMetadata.
   184→    val bootstrapDirectory = new BootstrapDirectory(config.metadataLogDir)
   185→    val bootstrapMetadata = bootstrapDirectory.read()
   186→    (metaPropsEnsemble, bootstrapMetadata)
   187→  }
   188→
   189→  val configSchema = new KafkaConfigSchema(Map(
   190→    ConfigResource.Type.BROKER -> new ConfigDef(KafkaConfig.configDef),
   191→    ConfigResource.Type.TOPIC -> LogConfig.configDefCopy,
   192→  ).asJava, ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.ALL_TOPIC_CONFIG_SYNONYMS)
   193→}
   194→

    18→package kafka.server
    19→
    20→import kafka.log.LogManager
    21→import kafka.network.SocketServer
    22→import kafka.utils.Logging
    23→import org.apache.kafka.common.ClusterResource

    39→
    40→import java.time.Duration
    41→import scala.collection.Seq
    42→import scala.jdk.CollectionConverters._
    43→
    44→object KafkaBroker {
    45→  //properties for MetricsContext
    46→  private val MetricsTypeName: String = "KafkaServer"
    47→
    48→  private[server] def notifyClusterListeners(clusterId: String,
    49→                                             clusterListeners: Seq[AnyRef]): Unit = {
    50→    val clusterResourceListeners = new ClusterResourceListeners
    51→    clusterResourceListeners.maybeAddAll(clusterListeners.asJava)
    52→    clusterResourceListeners.onUpdate(new ClusterResource(clusterId))
    53→  }
    54→
    55→  private[server] def notifyMetricsReporters(clusterId: String,
    56→                                             config: KafkaConfig,
    57→                                             metricsReporters: Seq[AnyRef]): Unit = {
    58→    val metricsContext = Server.createKafkaMetricsContext(config, clusterId)
    59→    metricsReporters.foreach {
    60→      case x: MetricsReporter => x.contextChange(metricsContext)
    61→      case _ => //do nothing
    62→    }
    63→  }
    64→
    65→  /**
    66→   * The log message that we print when the broker has been successfully started.
    67→   * The ducktape system tests look for a line matching the regex 'Kafka\s*Server.*started'
    68→   * to know when the broker is started, so it is best not to change this message -- but if
    69→   * you do change it, be sure to make it match that regex or the system tests will fail.
    70→   */
    71→  val STARTED_MESSAGE = "Kafka Server started"
    72→
    73→  val MIN_INCREMENTAL_FETCH_SESSION_EVICTION_MS: Long = 120000
    74→}
    75→
    76→trait KafkaBroker extends Logging {
    77→  // Number of shards to split FetchSessionCache into. This is to reduce contention when trying to
    78→  // acquire lock while handling Fetch requests.
    79→  val NumFetchSessionCacheShards: Int = 8
    80→
    81→  def authorizerPlugin: Option[Plugin[Authorizer]]
    82→  def brokerState: BrokerState
    83→  def clusterId: String
    84→  def config: KafkaConfig
    85→  def dataPlaneRequestHandlerPool: KafkaRequestHandlerPool
    86→  def dataPlaneRequestProcessor: KafkaApis
    87→  def kafkaScheduler: Scheduler
    88→  def kafkaYammerMetrics: KafkaYammerMetrics
    89→  def logDirFailureChannel: LogDirFailureChannel
    90→  def logManager: LogManager
    91→  def remoteLogManagerOpt: Option[RemoteLogManager]
    92→  def metrics: Metrics
    93→  def quotaManagers: QuotaFactory.QuotaManagers
    94→  def replicaManager: ReplicaManager
    95→  def socketServer: SocketServer
    96→  def metadataCache: MetadataCache
    97→  def groupCoordinator: GroupCoordinator
    98→  def boundPort(listenerName: ListenerName): Int

 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
 * limitations under the License.

package kafka.server

import kafka.log.LogManager
import kafka.network.SocketServer
import kafka.utils.Logging
import org.apache.kafka.common.ClusterResource
import org.apache.kafka.common.internals.{ClusterResourceListeners, Plugin}
import org.apache.kafka.common.metrics.{Metrics, MetricsReporter}
import org.apache.kafka.common.network.ListenerName
import org.apache.kafka.common.security.token.delegation.internals.DelegationTokenCache
import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Time
import org.apache.kafka.coordinator.group.GroupCoordinator
import org.apache.kafka.metadata.{BrokerState, MetadataCache}
import org.apache.kafka.security.CredentialProvider
import org.apache.kafka.server.authorizer.Authorizer
import org.apache.kafka.server.common.NodeToControllerChannelManager
import org.apache.kafka.server.log.remote.storage.RemoteLogManager
import org.apache.kafka.server.metrics.{KafkaMetricsGroup, KafkaYammerMetrics, LinuxIoMetricsCollector}
import org.apache.kafka.server.util.Scheduler
import org.apache.kafka.storage.internals.log.LogDirFailureChannel
import org.apache.kafka.storage.log.metrics.BrokerTopicStats

import java.time.Duration
import scala.collection.Seq
import scala.jdk.CollectionConverters._

object KafkaBroker {
  //properties for MetricsContext
  private val MetricsTypeName: String = "KafkaServer"

  private[server] def notifyClusterListeners(clusterId: String,
                                             clusterListeners: Seq[AnyRef]): Unit = {
    val clusterResourceListeners = new ClusterResourceListeners
    clusterResourceListeners.maybeAddAll(clusterListeners.asJava)
    clusterResourceListeners.onUpdate(new ClusterResource(clusterId))
  }

  private[server] def notifyMetricsReporters(clusterId: String,
                                             config: KafkaConfig,
                                             metricsReporters: Seq[AnyRef]): Unit = {
    val metricsContext = Server.createKafkaMetricsContext(config, clusterId)
    metricsReporters.foreach {
      case x: MetricsReporter => x.contextChange(metricsContext)
      case _ => //do nothing
    }
  }

  /**
   * The log message that we print when the broker has been successfully started.
   * The ducktape system tests look for a line matching the regex 'Kafka\s*Server.*started'
   * to know when the broker is started, so it is best not to change this message -- but if
   * you do change it, be sure to make it match that regex or the system tests will fail.
   */
  val STARTED_MESSAGE = "Kafka Server started"

  val MIN_INCREMENTAL_FETCH_SESSION_EVICTION_MS: Long = 120000
}

trait KafkaBroker extends Logging {
  // Number of shards to split FetchSessionCache into. This is to reduce contention when trying to
  // acquire lock while handling Fetch requests.
  val NumFetchSessionCacheShards: Int = 8

  def authorizerPlugin: Option[Plugin[Authorizer]]
  def brokerState: BrokerState
  def clusterId: String
  def config: KafkaConfig
  def dataPlaneRequestHandlerPool: KafkaRequestHandlerPool
  def dataPlaneRequestProcessor: KafkaApis
  def kafkaScheduler: Scheduler
  def kafkaYammerMetrics: KafkaYammerMetrics
  def logDirFailureChannel: LogDirFailureChannel
  def logManager: LogManager
  def remoteLogManagerOpt: Option[RemoteLogManager]
  def metrics: Metrics
  def quotaManagers: QuotaFactory.QuotaManagers
  def replicaManager: ReplicaManager
  def socketServer: SocketServer
  def metadataCache: MetadataCache
  def groupCoordinator: GroupCoordinator
  def boundPort(listenerName: ListenerName): Int
  def startup(): Unit
  def awaitShutdown(): Unit

100→  val metadataPublishers: util.List[MetadataPublisher] = new util.ArrayList[MetadataPublisher]()
   101→  @volatile var metadataCache : KRaftMetadataCache = _
   102→  @volatile var metadataCachePublisher: KRaftMetadataCachePublisher = _
   103→  @volatile var featuresPublisher: FeaturesPublisher = _
   104→  @volatile var registrationsPublisher: ControllerRegistrationsPublisher = _
   105→  @volatile var incarnationId: Uuid = _
   106→  @volatile var registrationManager: ControllerRegistrationManager = _
   107→  @volatile var registrationChannelManager: NodeToControllerChannelManager = _
   108→
   109→  private def maybeChangeStatus(from: ProcessStatus, to: ProcessStatus): Boolean = {
   110→    lock.lock()
   111→    try {
   112→      if (status != from) return false
   113→      status = to
   114→      if (to == SHUTDOWN) awaitShutdownCond.signalAll()
   115→    } finally {
   116→      lock.unlock()
   117→    }
   118→    true
   119→  }
   120→
   121→  def clusterId: String = sharedServer.clusterId
   122→
   123→  def startup(): Unit = {
   124→    if (!maybeChangeStatus(SHUTDOWN, STARTING)) return
   125→    val startupDeadline = Deadline.fromDelay(time, config.serverMaxStartupTimeMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)
   126→    try {
   127→      this.logIdent = logContext.logPrefix()
   128→      info("Starting controller")
   129→      config.dynamicConfig.initialize(clientMetricsReceiverPluginOpt = None)
   130→
   131→      maybeChangeStatus(STARTING, STARTED)
   132→
   133→      metricsGroup.newGauge("ClusterId", () => clusterId)
   134→      metricsGroup.newGauge("yammer-metrics-count", () =>  KafkaYammerMetrics.defaultRegistry.allMetrics.size)
   135→
   136→      linuxIoMetricsCollector = new LinuxIoMetricsCollector("/proc", time)
   137→      if (linuxIoMetricsCollector.usable()) {
   138→        metricsGroup.newGauge("linux-disk-read-bytes", () => linuxIoMetricsCollector.readBytes())
   139→        metricsGroup.newGauge("linux-disk-write-bytes", () => linuxIoMetricsCollector.writeBytes())
   140→      }
   141→
   142→      authorizerPlugin = config.createNewAuthorizer(metrics, ProcessRole.ControllerRole.toString)
   143→
   144→      metadataCache = new KRaftMetadataCache(config.nodeId, () => raftManager.client.kraftVersion())
   145→
   146→      metadataCachePublisher = new KRaftMetadataCachePublisher(metadataCache)
   147→
   148→      featuresPublisher = new FeaturesPublisher(logContext)
   149→
   150→      registrationsPublisher = new ControllerRegistrationsPublisher()
   151→
   152→      incarnationId = Uuid.randomUuid()
   153→
   154→      val apiVersionManager = new SimpleApiVersionManager(
   155→        ListenerType.CONTROLLER,
   156→        config.unstableApiVersionsEnabled,

val metadataPublishers: util.List[MetadataPublisher] = new util.ArrayList[MetadataPublisher]()
  @volatile var metadataCache : KRaftMetadataCache = _
  @volatile var metadataCachePublisher: KRaftMetadataCachePublisher = _
  @volatile var featuresPublisher: FeaturesPublisher = _
  @volatile var registrationsPublisher: ControllerRegistrationsPublisher = _
  @volatile var incarnationId: Uuid = _
  @volatile var registrationManager: ControllerRegistrationManager = _
  @volatile var registrationChannelManager: NodeToControllerChannelManager = _

  private def maybeChangeStatus(from: ProcessStatus, to: ProcessStatus): Boolean = {
    lock.lock()
    try {
      if (status != from) return false
      status = to
      if (to == SHUTDOWN) awaitShutdownCond.signalAll()
    } finally {
      lock.unlock()
    }
    true
  }

  def clusterId: String = sharedServer.clusterId

  def startup(): Unit = {
    if (!maybeChangeStatus(SHUTDOWN, STARTING)) return
    val startupDeadline = Deadline.fromDelay(time, config.serverMaxStartupTimeMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)
    try {
      this.logIdent = logContext.logPrefix()
      info("Starting controller")
      config.dynamicConfig.initialize(clientMetricsReceiverPluginOpt = None)

      maybeChangeStatus(STARTING, STARTED)

      metricsGroup.newGauge("ClusterId", () => clusterId)
      metricsGroup.newGauge("yammer-metrics-count", () =>  KafkaYammerMetrics.defaultRegistry.allMetrics.size)

      linuxIoMetricsCollector = new LinuxIoMetricsCollector("/proc", time)
      if (linuxIoMetricsCollector.usable()) {
        metricsGroup.newGauge("linux-disk-read-bytes", () => linuxIoMetricsCollector.readBytes())
        metricsGroup.newGauge("linux-disk-write-bytes", () => linuxIoMetricsCollector.writeBytes())
      }

      authorizerPlugin = config.createNewAuthorizer(metrics, ProcessRole.ControllerRole.toString)

      metadataCache = new KRaftMetadataCache(config.nodeId, () => raftManager.client.kraftVersion())

      metadataCachePublisher = new KRaftMetadataCachePublisher(metadataCache)

      featuresPublisher = new FeaturesPublisher(logContext)

      registrationsPublisher = new ControllerRegistrationsPublisher()

      incarnationId = Uuid.randomUuid()

      val apiVersionManager = new SimpleApiVersionManager(
        ListenerType.CONTROLLER,
        config.unstableApiVersionsEnabled,
        () => featuresPublisher.features().setFinalizedLevel(
          KRaftVersion.FEATURE_NAME,
          raftManager.client.kraftVersion().featureLevel())
      )

      //  metrics will be set to null when closing a controller, so we should recreate it for testing
      if (sharedServer.metrics == null){
        sharedServer.metrics = new Metrics()
      }

      tokenCache = new DelegationTokenCache(ScramMechanism.mechanismNames)
      credentialProvider = new CredentialProvider(ScramMechanism.mechanismNames, tokenCache)
      socketServer = new SocketServer(config,
        metrics,
        time,
        credentialProvider,
        apiVersionManager,
        sharedServer.socketFactory)

      val listenerInfo = ListenerInfo
        .create(config.effectiveAdvertisedControllerListeners.asJava)
        .withWildcardHostnamesResolved()
        .withEphemeralPortsCorrected(name => socketServer.boundPort(new ListenerName(name)))
      socketServerFirstBoundPortFuture.complete(listenerInfo.firstListener().port())

      val endpointReadyFutures = {
        val builder = new EndpointReadyFutures.Builder()
        builder.build(authorizerPlugin.toJava,
          new KafkaAuthorizerServerInfo(
            new ClusterResource(clusterId),
            config.nodeId,
            listenerInfo.listeners().values(),
            listenerInfo.firstListener(),
            config.earlyStartListeners.map(_.value()).asJava))
      }

      sharedServer.startForController(listenerInfo)

      createTopicPolicy = Option(config.
        getConfiguredInstance(CREATE_TOPIC_POLICY_CLASS_NAME_CONFIG, classOf[CreateTopicPolicy]))
      alterConfigPolicy = Option(config.
        getConfiguredInstance(ALTER_CONFIG_POLICY_CLASS_NAME_CONFIG, classOf[AlterConfigPolicy]))

      val voterConnections = FutureUtils.waitWithLogging(logger.underlying, logIdent,
        "controller quorum voters future",
        sharedServer.controllerQuorumVotersFuture,
        startupDeadline, time)
      val controllerNodes = QuorumConfig.voterConnectionsToNodes(voterConnections)
      val quorumFeatures = new QuorumFeatures(config.nodeId,
        QuorumFeatures.defaultSupportedFeatureMap(config.unstableFeatureVersionsEnabled),
        controllerNodes.asScala.map(node => Integer.valueOf(node.id())).asJava)

      val delegationTokenManagerConfigs = new DelegationTokenManagerConfigs(config)
      val delegationTokenKeyString = {
        if (delegationTokenManagerConfigs.tokenAuthEnabled) {
          delegationTokenManagerConfigs.delegationTokenSecretKey.value
        } else {
          null
        }
      }

      val controllerBuilder = {
        val leaderImbalanceCheckIntervalNs = if (config.autoLeaderRebalanceEnable) {
          OptionalLong.of(TimeUnit.NANOSECONDS.convert(config.leaderImbalanceCheckIntervalSeconds, TimeUnit.SECONDS))
        } else {
          OptionalLong.empty()
        }

        val maxIdleIntervalNs = config.metadataMaxIdleIntervalNs.fold(OptionalLong.empty)(OptionalLong.of)

        quorumControllerMetrics = new QuorumControllerMetrics(Optional.of(KafkaYammerMetrics.defaultRegistry), time, config.brokerSessionTimeoutMs)

        new QuorumController.Builder(config.nodeId, sharedServer.clusterId).
          setTime(time).
          setThreadNamePrefix(s"quorum-controller-${config.nodeId}-").
          setConfigSchema(configSchema).
          setRaftClient(raftManager.client).
          setQuorumFeatures(quorumFeatures).
          setDefaultReplicationFactor(config.defaultReplicationFactor.toShort).
          setDefaultNumPartitions(config.numPartitions.intValue()).
          setSessionTimeoutNs(TimeUnit.NANOSECONDS.convert(config.brokerSessionTimeoutMs.longValue(),
            TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)).
          setLeaderImbalanceCheckIntervalNs(leaderImbalanceCheckIntervalNs).
          setMaxIdleIntervalNs(maxIdleIntervalNs).
          setMetrics(quorumControllerMetrics).
          setCreateTopicPolicy(createTopicPolicy.toJava).
          setAlterConfigPolicy(alterConfigPolicy.toJava).
          setConfigurationValidator(new ControllerConfigurationValidator(sharedServer.brokerConfig)).
          setStaticConfig(config.originals).
          setBootstrapMetadata(bootstrapMetadata).
          setFatalFaultHandler(sharedServer.fatalQuorumControllerFaultHandler).
          setNonFatalFaultHandler(sharedServer.nonFatalQuorumControllerFaultHandler).
          setDelegationTokenCache(tokenCache).
          setDelegationTokenSecretKey(delegationTokenKeyString).
          setDelegationTokenMaxLifeMs(delegationTokenManagerConfigs.delegationTokenMaxLifeMs).
          setDelegationTokenExpiryTimeMs(delegationTokenManagerConfigs.delegationTokenExpiryTimeMs).
          setDelegationTokenExpiryCheckIntervalMs(delegationTokenManagerConfigs.delegationTokenExpiryCheckIntervalMs).
          setUncleanLeaderElectionCheckIntervalMs(config.uncleanLeaderElectionCheckIntervalMs).
          setControllerPerformanceSamplePeriodMs(config.controllerPerformanceSamplePeriodMs).
          setControllerPerformanceAlwaysLogThresholdMs(config.controllerPerformanceAlwaysLogThresholdMs)
      }
      controller = controllerBuilder.build()

      // If we are using a ClusterMetadataAuthorizer, requests to add or remove ACLs must go
      // through the controller.
      authorizerPlugin.foreach { plugin =>
        plugin.get match {
          case a: ClusterMetadataAuthorizer => a.setAclMutator(controller)
          case _ =>
        }
      }

      quotaManagers = QuotaFactory.instantiate(config,
        metrics,
        time,
        s"controller-${config.nodeId}-", ProcessRole.ControllerRole.toString)
      clientQuotaMetadataManager = new ClientQuotaMetadataManager(quotaManagers, socketServer.connectionQuotas)
      controllerApis = new ControllerApis(socketServer.dataPlaneRequestChannel,
        authorizerPlugin,
        quotaManagers,
        time,
        controller,
        raftManager,
        config,
        clusterId,
        registrationsPublisher,
        apiVersionManager,
        metadataCache)
      controllerApisHandlerPool = new KafkaRequestHandlerPool(config.nodeId,
        socketServer.dataPlaneRequestChannel,
        controllerApis,
        time,
        config.numIoThreads,
        "RequestHandlerAvgIdlePercent",
        "controller")

      // Set up the metadata cache publisher.
      metadataPublishers.add(metadataCachePublisher)

      // Set up the metadata features publisher.
      metadataPublishers.add(featuresPublisher)

      // Set up the controller registrations publisher.
      metadataPublishers.add(registrationsPublisher)

      // Create the registration manager, which handles sending KIP-919 controller registrations.
      registrationManager = new ControllerRegistrationManager(config.nodeId,
        clusterId,
        time,
        s"controller-${config.nodeId}-",
        QuorumFeatures.defaultSupportedFeatureMap(config.unstableFeatureVersionsEnabled),
        incarnationId,
        listenerInfo)

      // Add the registration manager to the list of metadata publishers, so that it receives
      // callbacks when the cluster registrations change.
      metadataPublishers.add(registrationManager)

      // Set up the dynamic config publisher. This runs even in combined mode, since the broker
      // has its own separate dynamic configuration object.
      metadataPublishers.add(new DynamicConfigPublisher(
        config,
        sharedServer.metadataPublishingFaultHandler,
        immutable.Map[ConfigType, ConfigHandler](
          // controllers don't host topics, so no need to do anything with dynamic topic config changes here
          ConfigType.BROKER -> new BrokerConfigHandler(config, quotaManagers)
        ),
        "controller"))

      // Register this instance for dynamic config changes to the KafkaConfig. This must be called
      // after the authorizer and quotaManagers are initialized, since it references those objects.
      // It must be called before DynamicClientQuotaPublisher is installed, since otherwise we may
      // miss the initial update which establishes the dynamic configurations that are in effect on
      // startup.
      config.dynamicConfig.addReconfigurables(this)

      // Set up the client quotas publisher. This will enable controller mutation quotas and any
      // other quotas which are applicable.
      metadataPublishers.add(new DynamicClientQuotaPublisher(
        config,
        sharedServer.metadataPublishingFaultHandler,
        "controller",
        clientQuotaMetadataManager
      ))

      // Set up the DynamicTopicClusterQuotaPublisher. This will enable quotas for the cluster and topics.
      metadataPublishers.add(new DynamicTopicClusterQuotaPublisher(
        clusterId,
        config,
        sharedServer.metadataPublishingFaultHandler,
        "controller",
        quotaManagers,
      ))

      // Set up the SCRAM publisher.
      metadataPublishers.add(new ScramPublisher(
        config,
        sharedServer.metadataPublishingFaultHandler,
        "controller",
        credentialProvider
      ))

      // Set up the DelegationToken publisher.
      // We need a tokenManager for the Publisher
      // The tokenCache in the tokenManager is the same used in DelegationTokenControlManager
      metadataPublishers.add(new DelegationTokenPublisher(
          config,
          sharedServer.metadataPublishingFaultHandler,
          "controller",
          new DelegationTokenManager(delegationTokenManagerConfigs, tokenCache)
      ))

      // Set up the metrics publisher.
      metadataPublishers.add(new ControllerMetadataMetricsPublisher(
        sharedServer.controllerServerMetrics,
        sharedServer.metadataPublishingFaultHandler
      ))

      // Set up the ACL publisher.
      metadataPublishers.add(new AclPublisher(
        config.nodeId,
        sharedServer.metadataPublishingFaultHandler,
        "controller",
        authorizerPlugin.toJava
      ))

      // Install all metadata publishers.
      FutureUtils.waitWithLogging(logger.underlying, logIdent,
        "the controller metadata publishers to be installed",
        sharedServer.loader.installPublishers(metadataPublishers), startupDeadline, time)

      val authorizerFutures: Map[Endpoint, CompletableFuture[Void]] = endpointReadyFutures.futures().asScala.toMap

      /**
       * Enable the controller endpoint(s). If we are using an authorizer which stores
       * ACLs in the metadata log, such as StandardAuthorizer, we will be able to start
       * accepting requests from principals included super.users right after this point,
       * but we will not be able to process requests from non-superusers until AclPublisher
       * publishes metadata from the QuorumController. MetadataPublishers do not publish
       * metadata until the controller has caught up to the high watermark.
       */
      val socketServerFuture = socketServer.enableRequestProcessing(authorizerFutures)

val metadataPublishers: util.List[MetadataPublisher] = new util.ArrayList[MetadataPublisher]()
  @volatile var metadataCache : KRaftMetadataCache = _
  @volatile var metadataCachePublisher: KRaftMetadataCachePublisher = _
  @volatile var featuresPublisher: FeaturesPublisher = _
  @volatile var registrationsPublisher: ControllerRegistrationsPublisher = _
  @volatile var incarnationId: Uuid = _
  @volatile var registrationManager: ControllerRegistrationManager = _
  @volatile var registrationChannelManager: NodeToControllerChannelManager = _

  private def maybeChangeStatus(from: ProcessStatus, to: ProcessStatus): Boolean = {
    lock.lock()
    try {
      if (status != from) return false
      status = to
      if (to == SHUTDOWN) awaitShutdownCond.signalAll()
    } finally {
      lock.unlock()
    }
    true
  }

  def clusterId: String = sharedServer.clusterId

  def startup(): Unit = {
    if (!maybeChangeStatus(SHUTDOWN, STARTING)) return
    val startupDeadline = Deadline.fromDelay(time, config.serverMaxStartupTimeMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)
    try {
      this.logIdent = logContext.logPrefix()
      info("Starting controller")
      config.dynamicConfig.initialize(clientMetricsReceiverPluginOpt = None)

      maybeChangeStatus(STARTING, STARTED)

      metricsGroup.newGauge("ClusterId", () => clusterId)
      metricsGroup.newGauge("yammer-metrics-count", () =>  KafkaYammerMetrics.defaultRegistry.allMetrics.size)

      linuxIoMetricsCollector = new LinuxIoMetricsCollector("/proc", time)
      if (linuxIoMetricsCollector.usable()) {
        metricsGroup.newGauge("linux-disk-read-bytes", () => linuxIoMetricsCollector.readBytes())
        metricsGroup.newGauge("linux-disk-write-bytes", () => linuxIoMetricsCollector.writeBytes())
      }

      authorizerPlugin = config.createNewAuthorizer(metrics, ProcessRole.ControllerRole.toString)

      metadataCache = new KRaftMetadataCache(config.nodeId, () => raftManager.client.kraftVersion())

      metadataCachePublisher = new KRaftMetadataCachePublisher(metadataCache)

      featuresPublisher = new FeaturesPublisher(logContext)

      registrationsPublisher = new ControllerRegistrationsPublisher()

      incarnationId = Uuid.randomUuid()

      val apiVersionManager = new SimpleApiVersionManager(
        ListenerType.CONTROLLER,
        config.unstableApiVersionsEnabled,
        () => featuresPublisher.features().setFinalizedLevel(
          KRaftVersion.FEATURE_NAME,
          raftManager.client.kraftVersion().featureLevel())
      )

      //  metrics will be set to null when closing a controller, so we should recreate it for testing
      if (sharedServer.metrics == null){
        sharedServer.metrics = new Metrics()
      }

      tokenCache = new DelegationTokenCache(ScramMechanism.mechanismNames)
      credentialProvider = new CredentialProvider(ScramMechanism.mechanismNames, tokenCache)
      socketServer = new SocketServer(config,
        metrics,
        time,
        credentialProvider,
        apiVersionManager,
        sharedServer.socketFactory)

      val listenerInfo = ListenerInfo
        .create(config.effectiveAdvertisedControllerListeners.asJava)
        .withWildcardHostnamesResolved()
        .withEphemeralPortsCorrected(name => socketServer.boundPort(new ListenerName(name)))
      socketServerFirstBoundPortFuture.complete(listenerInfo.firstListener().port())

      val endpointReadyFutures = {
        val builder = new EndpointReadyFutures.Builder()
        builder.build(authorizerPlugin.toJava,
          new KafkaAuthorizerServerInfo(
            new ClusterResource(clusterId),
            config.nodeId,
            listenerInfo.listeners().values(),
            listenerInfo.firstListener(),
            config.earlyStartListeners.map(_.value()).asJava))
      }

      sharedServer.startForController(listenerInfo)

      createTopicPolicy = Option(config.
        getConfiguredInstance(CREATE_TOPIC_POLICY_CLASS_NAME_CONFIG, classOf[CreateTopicPolicy]))
      alterConfigPolicy = Option(config.
        getConfiguredInstance(ALTER_CONFIG_POLICY_CLASS_NAME_CONFIG, classOf[AlterConfigPolicy]))

      val voterConnections = FutureUtils.waitWithLogging(logger.underlying, logIdent,
        "controller quorum voters future",
        sharedServer.controllerQuorumVotersFuture,
        startupDeadline, time)
      val controllerNodes = QuorumConfig.voterConnectionsToNodes(voterConnections)
      val quorumFeatures = new QuorumFeatures(config.nodeId,
        QuorumFeatures.defaultSupportedFeatureMap(config.unstableFeatureVersionsEnabled),
        controllerNodes.asScala.map(node => Integer.valueOf(node.id())).asJava)

      val delegationTokenManagerConfigs = new DelegationTokenManagerConfigs(config)
      val delegationTokenKeyString = {
        if (delegationTokenManagerConfigs.tokenAuthEnabled) {
          delegationTokenManagerConfigs.delegationTokenSecretKey.value
        } else {
          null
        }
      }

      val controllerBuilder = {
        val leaderImbalanceCheckIntervalNs = if (config.autoLeaderRebalanceEnable) {
          OptionalLong.of(TimeUnit.NANOSECONDS.convert(config.leaderImbalanceCheckIntervalSeconds, TimeUnit.SECONDS))
        } else {
          OptionalLong.empty()
        }

        val maxIdleIntervalNs = config.metadataMaxIdleIntervalNs.fold(OptionalLong.empty)(OptionalLong.of)

        quorumControllerMetrics = new QuorumControllerMetrics(Optional.of(KafkaYammerMetrics.defaultRegistry), time, config.brokerSessionTimeoutMs)

        new QuorumController.Builder(config.nodeId, sharedServer.clusterId).
          setTime(time).
          setThreadNamePrefix(s"quorum-controller-${config.nodeId}-").
          setConfigSchema(configSchema).
          setRaftClient(raftManager.client).
          setQuorumFeatures(quorumFeatures).
          setDefaultReplicationFactor(config.defaultReplicationFactor.toShort).
          setDefaultNumPartitions(config.numPartitions.intValue()).
          setSessionTimeoutNs(TimeUnit.NANOSECONDS.convert(config.brokerSessionTimeoutMs.longValue(),
            TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)).
          setLeaderImbalanceCheckIntervalNs(leaderImbalanceCheckIntervalNs).
          setMaxIdleIntervalNs(maxIdleIntervalNs).
          setMetrics(quorumControllerMetrics).
          setCreateTopicPolicy(createTopicPolicy.toJava).
          setAlterConfigPolicy(alterConfigPolicy.toJava).
          setConfigurationValidator(new ControllerConfigurationValidator(sharedServer.brokerConfig)).
          setStaticConfig(config.originals).
          setBootstrapMetadata(bootstrapMetadata).
          setFatalFaultHandler(sharedServer.fatalQuorumControllerFaultHandler).
          setNonFatalFaultHandler(sharedServer.nonFatalQuorumControllerFaultHandler).
          setDelegationTokenCache(tokenCache).
          setDelegationTokenSecretKey(delegationTokenKeyString).
          setDelegationTokenMaxLifeMs(delegationTokenManagerConfigs.delegationTokenMaxLifeMs).
          setDelegationTokenExpiryTimeMs(delegationTokenManagerConfigs.delegationTokenExpiryTimeMs).
          setDelegationTokenExpiryCheckIntervalMs(delegationTokenManagerConfigs.delegationTokenExpiryCheckIntervalMs).
          setUncleanLeaderElectionCheckIntervalMs(config.uncleanLeaderElectionCheckIntervalMs).
          setControllerPerformanceSamplePeriodMs(config.controllerPerformanceSamplePeriodMs).
          setControllerPerformanceAlwaysLogThresholdMs(config.controllerPerformanceAlwaysLogThresholdMs)
      }
      controller = controllerBuilder.build()

      // If we are using a ClusterMetadataAuthorizer, requests to add or remove ACLs must go
      // through the controller.
      authorizerPlugin.foreach { plugin =>
        plugin.get match {
          case a: ClusterMetadataAuthorizer => a.setAclMutator(controller)
          case _ =>
        }
      }

      quotaManagers = QuotaFactory.instantiate(config,
        metrics,
        time,
        s"controller-${config.nodeId}-", ProcessRole.ControllerRole.toString)
      clientQuotaMetadataManager = new ClientQuotaMetadataManager(quotaManagers, socketServer.connectionQuotas)
      controllerApis = new ControllerApis(socketServer.dataPlaneRequestChannel,
        authorizerPlugin,
        quotaManagers,
        time,
        controller,
        raftManager,
        config,
        clusterId,
        registrationsPublisher,
        apiVersionManager,
        metadataCache)
      controllerApisHandlerPool = new KafkaRequestHandlerPool(config.nodeId,
        socketServer.dataPlaneRequestChannel,
        controllerApis,
        time,
        config.numIoThreads,
        "RequestHandlerAvgIdlePercent",
        "controller")

      // Set up the metadata cache publisher.
      metadataPublishers.add(metadataCachePublisher)

      // Set up the metadata features publisher.
      metadataPublishers.add(featuresPublisher)

      // Set up the controller registrations publisher.
      metadataPublishers.add(registrationsPublisher)

      // Create the registration manager, which handles sending KIP-919 controller registrations.
      registrationManager = new ControllerRegistrationManager(config.nodeId,
        clusterId,
        time,
        s"controller-${config.nodeId}-",
        QuorumFeatures.defaultSupportedFeatureMap(config.unstableFeatureVersionsEnabled),
        incarnationId,
        listenerInfo)

      // Add the registration manager to the list of metadata publishers, so that it receives
      // callbacks when the cluster registrations change.
      metadataPublishers.add(registrationManager)

      // Set up the dynamic config publisher. This runs even in combined mode, since the broker
      // has its own separate dynamic configuration object.
      metadataPublishers.add(new DynamicConfigPublisher(
        config,
        sharedServer.metadataPublishingFaultHandler,
        immutable.Map[ConfigType, ConfigHandler](
          // controllers don't host topics, so no need to do anything with dynamic topic config changes here
          ConfigType.BROKER -> new BrokerConfigHandler(config, quotaManagers)
        ),
        "controller"))

      // Register this instance for dynamic config changes to the KafkaConfig. This must be called
      // after the authorizer and quotaManagers are initialized, since it references those objects.
      // It must be called before DynamicClientQuotaPublisher is installed, since otherwise we may
      // miss the initial update which establishes the dynamic configurations that are in effect on
      // startup.
      config.dynamicConfig.addReconfigurables(this)

      // Set up the client quotas publisher. This will enable controller mutation quotas and any
      // other quotas which are applicable.
      metadataPublishers.add(new DynamicClientQuotaPublisher(
        config,
        sharedServer.metadataPublishingFaultHandler,
        "controller",
        clientQuotaMetadataManager
      ))

      // Set up the DynamicTopicClusterQuotaPublisher. This will enable quotas for the cluster and topics.
      metadataPublishers.add(new DynamicTopicClusterQuotaPublisher(
        clusterId,
        config,
        sharedServer.metadataPublishingFaultHandler,
        "controller",
        quotaManagers,
      ))

      // Set up the SCRAM publisher.
      metadataPublishers.add(new ScramPublisher(
        config,
        sharedServer.metadataPublishingFaultHandler,
        "controller",
        credentialProvider
      ))

      // Set up the DelegationToken publisher.
      // We need a tokenManager for the Publisher
      // The tokenCache in the tokenManager is the same used in DelegationTokenControlManager
      metadataPublishers.add(new DelegationTokenPublisher(
          config,
          sharedServer.metadataPublishingFaultHandler,
          "controller",
          new DelegationTokenManager(delegationTokenManagerConfigs, tokenCache)
      ))

      // Set up the metrics publisher.
      metadataPublishers.add(new ControllerMetadataMetricsPublisher(
        sharedServer.controllerServerMetrics,
        sharedServer.metadataPublishingFaultHandler
      ))

      // Set up the ACL publisher.
      metadataPublishers.add(new AclPublisher(
        config.nodeId,
        sharedServer.metadataPublishingFaultHandler,
        "controller",
        authorizerPlugin.toJava
      ))

      // Install all metadata publishers.
      FutureUtils.waitWithLogging(logger.underlying, logIdent,
        "the controller metadata publishers to be installed",
        sharedServer.loader.installPublishers(metadataPublishers), startupDeadline, time)

      val authorizerFutures: Map[Endpoint, CompletableFuture[Void]] = endpointReadyFutures.futures().asScala.toMap

      /**
       * Enable the controller endpoint(s). If we are using an authorizer which stores
       * ACLs in the metadata log, such as StandardAuthorizer, we will be able to start
       * accepting requests from principals included super.users right after this point,
       * but we will not be able to process requests from non-superusers until AclPublisher
       * publishes metadata from the QuorumController. MetadataPublishers do not publish
       * metadata until the controller has caught up to the high watermark.
       */
      val socketServerFuture = socketServer.enableRequestProcessing(authorizerFutures)

   433→  def shutdown(): Unit = {
   434→    if (!maybeChangeStatus(STARTED, SHUTTING_DOWN)) return
   435→    try {
   436→      info("shutting down")
   437→      // Ensure that we're not the Raft leader prior to shutting down our socket server, for a
   438→      // smoother transition.
   439→      sharedServer.ensureNotRaftLeader()
   440→      incarnationId = null
   441→      Utils.closeQuietly(registrationManager, "registration manager")
   442→      registrationManager = null
   443→      if (registrationChannelManager != null) {
   444→        CoreUtils.swallow(registrationChannelManager.shutdown(), this)
   445→        registrationChannelManager = null
   446→      }
   447→      metadataPublishers.forEach(p => sharedServer.loader.removeAndClosePublisher(p).get())
   448→      metadataPublishers.clear()
   449→      if (metadataCache != null) {

/*

 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
 * limitations under the License.
 */

package org.apache.kafka.server.share.persister;

import java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture;

/**
 * This interface introduces methods which can be used by callers to interact with the
 * persistence implementation responsible for storing share group/partition states.
 * For KIP-932, the default {@link Persister} uses a share coordinator to store information in
 * an internal topic.
 */
public interface Persister {
    /**
     * Initialize the share partition state.
     *
     * @param request Request parameters
     * @return A {@link CompletableFuture} that completes with the result.
     */
    CompletableFuture<InitializeShareGroupStateResult> initializeState(InitializeShareGroupStateParameters request);

    /**
     * Read share-partition state.
     *
     * @param request Request parameters
     * @return A {@link CompletableFuture} that completes with the result.
     */
    CompletableFuture<ReadShareGroupStateResult> readState(ReadShareGroupStateParameters request);

    /**
     * Write share-partition state.
     *
     * @param request Request parameters
     * @return A {@link CompletableFuture} that completes with the result.
     */
    CompletableFuture<WriteShareGroupStateResult> writeState(WriteShareGroupStateParameters request);

    /**
     * Delete share-partition state.
     *
     * @param request Request parameters
     * @return A {@link CompletableFuture} that completes with the result.
     */
    CompletableFuture<DeleteShareGroupStateResult> deleteState(DeleteShareGroupStateParameters request);

    /**
     * Read the offset information from share-partition state.
     *
     * @param request Request parameters
     * @return A {@link CompletableFuture} that completes with the result.
     */
    CompletableFuture<ReadShareGroupStateSummaryResult> readSummary(ReadShareGroupStateSummaryParameters request);

    /**
     * Perform cleanup and interrupt any threads
     */
    void stop();
}

The start() method (line 223) initiates the broker's registration with the controller. The close() method (line 304) gracefully shuts down the lifecycle manager and its event queue.\n\nDependent services implementing the lifecycle pattern include:\n- TransactionCoordinator.scala: startup() and shutdown() for transaction management\n- TransactionStateManager.scala: startup() and shutdown() for transaction log state\n- LogManager.scala: startup() and shutdown() for log storage\n- SocketServer.scala: start() and shutdown() for network services\n- RaftManager.scala: startup() and shutdown() for Raft consensus\n- ReplicaManager.scala: startup() and shutdown() for partition replicas\n- AlterPartitionManager.scala: start() and shutdown()\n- NodeToControllerChannelManager (both Scala and Java versions): start() and shutdown()\n\nJava implementations include:\n- NodeToControllerChannelManager.java (server-common): interface with start() and shutdown()\n- KafkaScheduler.java: startup() and shutdown() for scheduled tasks\n- ShutdownableThread.java: abstract base for background threads\n- Persister.java: interface with stop() for state persistence\n\nThe shutdown sequence ensures dependent services stop in reverse order, preventing resource contention and data loss. Socket server stops processing requests before shutdown, coordinators are shut down before replicas, and the scheduler is stopped early to prevent concurrent operations on closing resources. This carefully orchestrated lifecycle management is critical for Kafka's reliability and data consistency."
